title: New TI Sensor Tech Drives Toward a Collision-Free Future, Advancing Autonomous Driving and Vehicle Safety
summary: Texas Instruments (TI) unveiled a new AWR2944 radar sensor at CES 2022, designed to enhance autonomous driving and vehicle safety. This sensor can detect objects up to 40% farther away, is 30% smaller, and offers 33% higher resolution than existing radar sensors, specifically improving blind spot monitoring and turn navigation. The technology aims to contribute to a "collision-free future" by addressing challenges in processing large amounts of data for advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) and improving visibility around corners, particularly important for automated parking and driving.

title: Micron Technology searches for site for $40B factory. Is Boise among the contenders?
summary: Micron Technology is reportedly scouting locations for a new $40 billion manufacturing plant, with sites in Arizona, California, Texas, and North Carolina under consideration, though Boise is apparently not on the list. This search is part of Micron's larger plan to invest over $150 billion in chip production over 10 years to address a global chip shortage and meet growing demand driven by AI, 5G, and autonomous vehicles. The company is evaluating locations based on factors like site availability, talent pool, utility services, sustainability, and regulatory environment, with a focus on domestic production potentially spurred by the CHIPS Act.

title: Optical lovebirds eye a $14 billion market opportunity
summary: The merger between ADTRAN and ADVA is nearing completion, with ADTRAN shareholders approving the deal. The combined company anticipates a significant increase in its total addressable market to almost $14 billion, driven by growth opportunities in North America and Europe, and expects to boost sales by 50% in a few years. Despite the challenges of integration, both CEOs are optimistic about building a global leader in fiber networking solutions.

title: Datto Hires Splunk, Qlik Veteran Brooke Cunningham as CMO -
summary: Datto has appointed Brooke Cunningham, a veteran from Splunk and Qlik, as its new Chief Marketing Officer. This strategic hire aligns with Datto's ongoing expansion beyond its traditional storage offerings into cloud-based software and security solutions for Managed Service Providers (MSPs). Cunningham's experience in scaling businesses and nurturing partner ecosystems, particularly in the security technology sector, is expected to support Datto's accelerated growth and its commitment to the MSP community.

title: Comcast Announces World-First Test of 10G Modem Technology Capable of Delivering Multigigabit Speeds to Homes
summary: Comcast successfully tested a prototype 10G modem using Full Duplex DOCSIS 4.0 technology, achieving upload and download speeds faster than 4 Gbps. This marks a significant milestone in Comcast's 10G initiative, demonstrating the capability to deliver multigigabit speeds over existing network connections and promising increased reliability, performance, and lower latency for customers.

title: DevOps Tool Market 2022 Outstanding Growth Scope Witnessed
summary: The global DevOps Tool market is projected to reach USD 6881.6 million by 2026, growing from USD 4092.1 million in 2020 at a CAGR of 13.9% over five years. This report by Infinity Business Insights analyzes key players, market trends, and the impact of COVID-19, providing insights for investors and businesses to achieve market growth. The study covers product types like DevOps Ready, Enabled, and Capable, and applications across various sectors including IT, Telecom, BFSI, and Government.

title: The Threat Landscape in 2021
summary: Symantec, a division of Broadcom Software, reviewed the major cyber threats of 2021, highlighting ransomware, supply chain attacks, exploitation of public-facing application vulnerabilities, and attacks on critical infrastructure. While ransomware attack numbers decreased, targeted ransomware incidents increased significantly, fueled by initial access brokers and ransomware-as-a-service models. The report details prominent attacks like SolarWinds, Kaseya, and Colonial Pipeline, emphasizing the evolving tactics of threat actors and the rising impact on organizations and society.

title: Siemens collaborates with UMC on design kits for automotive and power applications
summary: Siemens Digital Industries Software has partnered with United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) to create process design kits (PDKs) for UMC's 110-nanometer and 180-nanometer BCD technology platforms. These PDKs are optimized for Siemens EDA’s Tanner custom design flow software, enabling the creation of integrated circuits for automotive and power applications. The collaboration aims to provide customers with the necessary tools to develop sophisticated power management solutions amidst increasing market demand.

title: Silicon Labs Announces BG24, MG24 TinyML 2.4GHz SoCs — and Boasts of a Fourfold Performance Gain
summary: Silicon Labs has unveiled its new BG24 and MG24 system-on-chip (SoC) range, targeting edge AI projects with a claimed fourfold performance boost and six-times energy efficiency improvement. These SoCs feature an Arm Cortex-M33 processor, dedicated AI/ML accelerators, and support for TensorFlow workloads. The BG24 offers Bluetooth 5.3 Low Energy, while the MG24 adds OpenThread, Zigbee, and Matter compatibility, with general availability expected in April.

title: Tech firms connect for nature conservation
summary: Ericsson, PLDT Inc., and Smart Communications Inc. have partnered to use technology and AI-enabled solutions for the conservation of a 405-hectare mangrove wetland in Sasmuan, Pampanga. This collaboration aims to protect endangered wildlife and marine biodiversity in the area, which was designated an internationally important wetland under the Ramsar Convention in 2021. The project involves deploying AI-enabled CCTV cameras to identify bird species, with PLDT hosting the cloud system for data and Smart providing internet connectivity for environmental monitoring.

title: CRDO Stock Price, Forecast & Analysis | CREDO TECHNOLOGY GROUP HOLDI (NASDAQ:CRDO)
summary: The article provides a detailed analysis of CREDO Technology Group Holding (NASDAQ:CRDO), covering its stock price, forecast, and various analytical ratings. It highlights CRDO's strong performance in the past year, its technical and fundamental ratings from ChartMill, and analysts' expectations for significant future growth in both EPS and revenue. The company specializes in high-speed connectivity solutions for the data infrastructure market.

title: Juniper Guns for Cisco, Nokia With Revamped Routing Silicon
summary: Juniper Networks has announced new routing silicon, Express 5 and Trio 6, claiming superior performance and power efficiency compared to Cisco's and Nokia's offerings. The Express 5 chips, designed for core routing, offer up to 28.8 Tb/s throughput, while the Trio 6, for edge routing, features machine learning capabilities and a 70% improvement in power efficiency. Dell'Oro Group analyst Shin Umeda notes that direct comparison of ASICs is challenging due to varying vendor development cycles and deployment timelines.

title: Reference design offers wireless docking over Wi-Fi 6E for PCs
summary: Synaptics Incorporated has introduced Gemini, a reference design that offers wireless docking over Wi-Fi 6E for PCs, claiming it's the world's first true Wi-Fi 6/6E wireless docking experience for laptops. This technology enables full dual-4K display capability and ultra-low latency, comparable to wired connections, and integrates hardware and software components like the BCM43752 Wi-Fi 6/6E radio and DL-1950 video interface IC. While initially focused on PC docking, Gemini's applications extend to tablets, gaming, and VR, with analyst Patrick Moorhead highlighting its potential to revolutionize connectivity in hybrid work environments.

title: VAST Data Accelerates Global Expansion Plans Across Europe and Asia
summary: VAST Data, a storage software company, is expanding its global operations across Europe and Asia, following previous launches in several countries. This expansion aims to meet the growing demand for its Universal Storage platform, offering a modern alternative to traditional HDD storage systems, especially for AI computing, big data, and analytics. The company has partnered with leading distributors in various regions to support this growth.

title: Apple, Broadcom win new trial in $1.1 bln Caltech patent case
summary: A U.S. appeals court has invalidated a $1.1 billion jury verdict against Apple and Broadcom in a patent infringement case brought by the California Institute of Technology (Caltech). The court deemed the damages "legally unsupportable" and ordered a new trial on the amount of damages, while upholding the infringement findings for two of Caltech's patents. The case revolves around Caltech's Wi-Fi technology patents used in millions of Apple devices containing Broadcom chips.

title: Apple, Broadcom Win New Trial In $1.1BN Caltech Patent Case
summary: A United States appeals court has overturned a jury's decision that ordered Apple and Broadcom to pay $1.1 billion to Caltech for infringing on Wi-Fi technology patents, ordering a new trial on damages. The court deemed the original award "legally unsupportable" but upheld the finding of infringement on two patents and called for a new trial on a third. Caltech's two-tier damages model was rejected by the appeals court, which found it legally unsound.

title: Deepika Rayala
summary: Deepika Rayala, EVP, CIO (International Operations Centre) at Yext, is an experienced IT leader with over two decades of experience in IT transformation. She emphasizes the immense opportunities for technology leaders in leveraging digital acceleration while preserving human connections. Rayala has a strong background in building IT organizations and leading large transformation programs, holding degrees in Chemical Engineering and an MBA.

title: Intel to Buy Israel’s Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 Billion
summary: Intel Corp. has agreed to acquire Israel’s Tower Semiconductor Ltd. for $5.4 billion as part of CEO Pat Gelsinger’s strategy to expand into the outsourced chip-manufacturing business. This acquisition aims to enhance Intel's expertise and customer base in the chip-foundry market, directly competing with industry leaders like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. The deal is expected to close in about 12 months and is a key part of Intel's broader investment cycle to modernize its factories and rebuild manufacturing capabilities.

title: What’s Changed: 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Information Archiving
summary: The article analyzes the 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Information Archiving, highlighting the evolving market driven by digital communication and application data. It discusses changes in vendor inclusion criteria, significant movements of key players like Global Relay and Veritas Technologies into the Leaders quadrant, and the strengths of various providers across the Leaders, Visionaries, and Niche Players categories, including Microsoft, Smarsh, Proofpoint, Mimecast, Micro Focus, ZL, Archive360, Solix Technologies, Barracuda, and Jatheon.

title: Ukraine: Disk-wiping Attacks Precede Russian Invasion
summary: Symantec detected a new disk-wiping malware, Trojan.Killdisk, used in attacks against Ukrainian organizations and in Lithuania before Russia's invasion on February 24, 2022. The attacks targeted financial, defense, aviation, and IT sectors, with evidence suggesting preparations began in November 2021. In some instances, ransomware was deployed simultaneously as a decoy to mask the wiper attacks.

title: Intel Boosting Chipmaking With $5.4B Tower Buy
summary: Intel Corp. is set to acquire Tower Semiconductor Ltd. for $5.4 billion to expand its foundry business and meet chip demand. The acquisition aims to leverage Tower's expertise in manufacturing analog chips for various industries and scale Intel's foundry services globally. The deal, pending regulatory approval, is expected to enhance Intel's capacity in the growing foundry market.

title: The role of phase-change memory in automotive OTA firmware upgrades
summary: This article discusses the crucial role of phase-change memory (PCM) in enabling efficient and reliable over-the-air (OTA) firmware upgrades in automotive zone- and domain-based ECU architectures. It explains how PCM addresses challenges like memory cost, update speed, and downtime compared to traditional NOR flash by utilizing a unique dual-cell structure for single-ended mode upgrades, effectively doubling memory capacity during the process. The article highlights PCM's advantages, including faster write operations, no erase requirement, and low power consumption, making it ideal for the next generation of automotive ECUs.

title: GLOBALFOUNDRIES ANNOUNCES NEXT GENERATION IN SILICON PHOTONICS SOLUTIONS AND COLLABORATES WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS TO ADVANCE A NEW ERA OF MORE IN THE DATA CENTER
summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) has announced GF Fotonix™, its next-generation silicon photonics platform, aimed at addressing the explosive growth of data and reducing power consumption in data centers. GF is collaborating with industry leaders including Broadcom, Cisco Systems, Inc., Marvell, and NVIDIA, along with photonic innovators like Ayar Labs and PsiQuantum, to deliver innovative and feature-rich solutions. This monolithic platform, combining advanced photonics features and RF-CMOS on a single silicon wafer, is designed to enable faster, more energy-efficient data transmission and supports various high-growth markets.

title: FIT Introduces QSFP-DD800 Connector / DAC Solutions at OFC 2022
summary: Foxconn Interconnect Technology (FIT) announced that it is jointly exhibiting its QSFP-DD800 interconnection solution with Broadcom and MultiLane at the Optical Networking and Communication Conference & Exhibition (OFC) in San Diego. The solution aims to address the growing demand for higher bandwidth in communication technology (5G/6G) by enabling 800G transmission speeds, crucial for upgrading switch data rates and supporting cloud applications. FIT's QSFP-DD800 also incorporates optimization and immersion cooling technologies to promote lower energy and carbon consumption, assisting partners in achieving carbon neutrality.

title: CAFC Affirms ITC Denial of Broadcom’s Request for Ban on Renesas Products Under Section 337
summary: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) affirmed the ITC's decision to deny Broadcom's request for a ban on Renesas products. The ITC had denied the ban because Broadcom failed to satisfy the domestic industry requirement under Section 337, as it couldn't demonstrate an actual product protected by its patents. Additionally, the CAFC upheld the Patent Trial and Appeal Board's findings that certain claims of Broadcom's patents were obvious.

title: Here’s How ICE Illegally Obtained Bulk Financial Records from Western Union
summary: Senator Ron Wyden has revealed that Homeland Security Investigations (HSI), a unit within ICE, illegally obtained millions of financial records from Western Union and Maxitransfers Corporation using administrative subpoenas. These subpoenas compelled the companies to provide records of money transfers over $500 to or from certain U.S. states and Mexico, which were then stored in a database accessible to hundreds of law enforcement agencies. This bulk surveillance is deemed illegal due to the misuse of administrative subpoenas, which are statutorily required to seek records "relevant" to specific investigations.

title: Webroot sues CrowdStrike, Sophos, others over alleged patent infringement
summary: Webroot and its parent company OpenText are suing Trend Micro, Sophos, Kaspersky, and CrowdStrike for alleged infringement of several malware detection patents, some dating back to 2005. The lawsuits, filed in the Western District of Texas, claim these defendants infringe upon Webroot's intellectual property related to endpoint security products and platforms. Competitors and observers criticized the move, with CrowdStrike calling it typical of a "patent troll" and others viewing it as "patent bullying."

title: Israel to build high-power laser interceptor
summary: Israeli Defense Minister Benny Gantz has approved a multi-million shekel budget for the development and production of the “Iron Beam” high-power laser system. This system is designed to intercept rockets, mortars, and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), representing a significant technological breakthrough and enhancement to Israel's security capabilities. The project involves the IMOD Directorate of Defense Research and Development (DDR&D), Rafael Advanced Defense Systems Ltd., and Elbit Systems Ltd.

title: Global Navigation Satellite System Market is Projected to Hit USD 320.73 Billion in 2021-2028 | GNSS Industry Exhibit a CAGR of 9.02%
summary: The global navigation satellite system (GNSS) market is projected to grow from USD 175.19 billion in 2021 to USD 320.73 billion in 2028, exhibiting a CAGR of 9.02%. This growth is driven by numerous applications across various sectors and the increasing acceptance of autonomous vehicles. The COVID-19 pandemic has had an affirmative effect on the GNSS market due to boosted supply and demand.

title: Verblecon: Sophisticated New Loader Used in Low-level Attacks
summary: Symantec analysts discovered a new, complex malware loader named Trojan.Verblecon in January 2022, primarily used for installing cryptocurrency miners and potentially stealing Discord tokens. Despite its sophisticated capabilities, the current attacks are low-reward, suggesting the attackers may not fully grasp the malware's potential. If leveraged by more experienced actors, Verblecon could be used for more serious threats like ransomware or espionage campaigns.

title: Global Semiconductor Market to Boost in Coming Years –
summary: The global semiconductor market was valued at USD 550.2 billion in 2021 and is projected to reach USD 820.1 billion by 2028, growing at a CAGR of 5.3%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing demand for consumer electronic devices and the adoption of advanced technologies like AI and IoT, with the Asia-Pacific region dominating the market. Despite challenges from changing trade policies and high manufacturing costs, the market continues to expand due to surging demand for memory devices and the accelerating pace of digitization.

title: New secure elements authenticate Qi 1.3 wireless charging
summary: Microchip Technology has introduced new secure elements, TrustFLEX ECC608 and Trust Anchor TA100, to authenticate devices using Qi 1.3 wireless charging. These solutions, combined with Microchip's secure key provisioning services, aim to provide an all-in-one secure storage subsystem for consumer and automotive applications, meeting the WPC Qi 1.3 specification's mandate for cryptographic verification of power transmitters. This enables safer and faster wireless charging by authenticating the charger's source and quality.

title: Watkins Represents Broadcom Inc. in US$1.95 Billion Private Senior Notes Offering
summary: Latham & Watkins LLP represented Broadcom Inc. in its US$1.95 billion private senior notes offering. The offering includes US$750 million of 4.00% Senior Notes due 2029 and US$1.2 billion of 4.15% Senior Notes due 2032. The transaction is expected to settle on April 14, 2022.

title: Is QQQ A Good Buy For Dividend Portfolios? Why There Are Better Choices
summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust, Series 1 ETF (QQQ) has seen strong capital appreciation in recent years, largely due to factors like ultra-loose monetary policy and pandemic tailwinds. However, these factors are unlikely to persist, making QQQ less suitable for income-focused dividend portfolios. The author suggests investors might find better income opportunities by selecting individual stocks.

title: KKR to buy cybersecurity firm Barracuda from Thoma Bravo in deal worth about $4 bln
summary: KKR & Co Inc announced its plan to acquire cybersecurity firm Barracuda Networks from Thoma Bravo for approximately $4 billion, including debt. This deal highlights the ongoing strong interest in the cybersecurity sector from buyout firms, fueled by the increase in remote work accelerated by the pandemic and a rise in cyberattacks. Barracuda, founded in 2003, provides cloud-based data security services and generates over $500 million in annual revenue.

title: Broadcom launches Wi-Fi 7 chips
summary: Broadcom has introduced five new Wi-Fi 7 chips designed for residential, enterprise, and mobile applications, aiming to double Wi-Fi bandwidth and improve throughput, latency, and range. A key feature is advanced multi-link operation (MLO), which aggregates channels and rapidly switches between them to optimize data flow in congested networks. This advancement is expected to enhance demanding applications like 16K video streaming, real-time collaboration, and AR/VR, while supporting the growing demand for deterministic wireless data in smart homes and industrial IoT.

title: Lazarus Targets Chemical Sector
summary: The North Korea-linked Lazarus APT group has been observed targeting organizations in the chemical sector in an espionage campaign, a continuation of their "Operation Dream Job." The attacks involve luring victims with fake job offers to install malware. Symantec identified this activity in South Korea, primarily within the chemical sector, noting the use of Trojanized tools and sophisticated lateral movement and persistence techniques.

title: Broadcom Inc. stock falls Thursday, underperforms market
summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) stock fell 3.15% on Thursday, closing at $573.85, a day when the broader market also saw declines. This drop ended a two-day winning streak for the company, leaving its stock $103.91 short of its 52-week high. Competitors such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Ltd. ADR, NVIDIA Corp., and Intel Corp. also experienced declines.

title: Netflix Scores as California Judge Says Broadcom’s Dynamic Resource Provisioning Patent Claim is Abstract under Alice
summary: A U.S. District Judge in California has invalidated a Broadcom patent claim against Netflix, ruling it was an abstract idea under the Alice/Mayo framework. The patent, related to dynamic resource provisioning for job placement, was deemed no different from common task allocation, dealing a setback to Broadcom's patent enforcement campaign against Netflix. This decision is part of ongoing litigation where Broadcom asserts Netflix's streaming services infringe on its server technology patents, affecting Broadcom's traditional set-top box business.

title: Modern Bank Heists 5.0: The Escalation from Dwell to Destruction
summary: The fifth edition of the Modern Bank Heists report reveals a significant escalation in cyber threats targeting financial institutions, moving from "dwell to destruction." Cybercrime cartels are operating like multinational corporations, utilizing dark web economies and state protection to launch destructive attacks, ransomware, and economic espionage. The report emphasizes the need for increased security budgets and proactive measures like extended detection and response (XDR) and regular threat hunting to combat these evolving threats.

title: Nvidia's GPU-powered AI is creating chips with 'better than human design'
summary: Nvidia is leveraging its GPU-powered AI to develop and design new computer chips, including its own GPUs, leading to designs that are "better than human." The company's chief scientist, Bill Dally, explained how AI is used to accelerate chip design processes like power estimation, parasitic prediction, and automated standard cell migration. This approach not only significantly reduces design time and labor but also results in more efficient and smaller chip components, showcasing a win-win scenario for Nvidia's internal development and its AI hardware offerings.

title: Oi ends sale of mobile service, targets fiber
summary: Telecoms company Oi has finalized the sale of its mobile service operation, Oi Móvel, to Telefônica Brasil, TIM S/A, and América Móvil’s Claro Participações, bringing in R$14.47 billion. This move allows Oi to focus on its transformation plan, deleveraging debt, and investing in fiber-optic connectivity and digital services. The transaction, though questioned by competitors and consumer protection groups, was approved by regulators with conditioning measures.

title: Broadcom under Antitrust scrutiny from FTC again - The Information
summary: Semiconductor giant Broadcom Inc. is facing renewed scrutiny from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) over alleged exclusive agreements with customers. The FTC is investigating complaints that Broadcom is illegally forcing exclusivity, potentially leveraging supply-chain issues as justification. This comes after previous antitrust settlements with the FTC in 2021 and the European Commission in 2020 regarding similar practices.

title: ModalAI launches new autopilot in VOXL 2
summary: ModalAI has launched VOXL 2, a new autopilot powered by the Qualcomm Flight RB5 5G platform, designed for autonomous drones and robots. This 16-gram autopilot boasts high AI computing power, integrating a PX4 flight controller, an 8-core CPU, and 5G connectivity, and is assembled in the USA. VOXL 2 is a result of the Blue sUAS 2.0 initiative and aims to advance the U.S. drone industry by providing a secure and capable solution, especially for GPS-denied environments and beyond visual line-of-sight operations.

title: Top 10 Tech Stocks by Market Capitalization
summary: This article lists the top 10 tech stocks by market capitalization as of April 2022, providing details on each company's industry, market cap, and annualized returns. It also defines tech stocks, explains their characteristics, and discusses why they present significant investment opportunities, highlighting their innovation and growth potential.

title: Texas Instruments introduces new solid state relays for safer EVs
summary: Texas Instruments (TI) has launched a new portfolio of automotive-qualified solid-state relays designed to enhance the safety of electric vehicles (EVs). These new components, including the TPSI3050-Q1 isolated switch driver and TPSI2140-Q1 isolated switch, aim to provide industry-leading reliability, reduce solution size, and lower bill-of-materials costs for 800-V battery-management systems. The integrated isolation technology in these relays offers faster load disconnection and connection compared to electromechanical relays, improving overall system safety and reliability for high-voltage automotive and industrial applications.

title: Broadcom Vs. Marvell: Better Chipmaker Company To Buy (NASDAQ:AVGO)
summary: This article compares Broadcom (AVGO) and Marvell (MRVL) primarily focusing on their semiconductor businesses. It concludes that Marvell has an edge in ASICs and processor technology, while Broadcom excels in analog business and demonstrates a superior M&A strategy with better revenue contribution and margins. The analysis aims to determine the better chipmaker company to buy.

title: Uber Eats pilots autonomous delivery with Serve Robotics, Motional
summary: Uber Eats is launching two autonomous delivery pilots in Los Angeles with Serve Robotics and Motional. These programs are part of Uber's new product range and aim to explore customer and merchant preferences for autonomous delivery, with plans to scale successful models. The partnership with Serve Robotics, an Uber spinout, focuses on shorter trips, while Motional, mainly known for robotaxis, will handle longer distances.

title: CEO steps in as Progressive slammed for 'woke' hiring practices
summary: Progressive CEO Tricia Griffith defended the company's diversity, equity, and inclusion (DE&I) hiring practices after facing criticism from an activist shareholder at the annual meeting. The shareholder, associated with the Free Enterprise project, questioned the prioritization of "skin color and reproductive organs" over merit. Griffith affirmed that DE&I is crucial for growth and reflecting the diverse customer base.

title: Carousell, L Catterton SPAC Merger Talks End Amid Rout
summary: Carousell Pte, a Singapore-based online classifieds marketplace operator, has ended talks to go public through a merger with blank-check company L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp. The decision comes amid market volatility, macroeconomic uncertainty, and valuation concerns, making it difficult to arrange a private investment in public equity (PIPE). The special purpose acquisition company (SPAC), backed by L Catterton and minority-owned by LVMH, had been in exclusive talks for a transaction that could have valued the combined entity at $1.5 billion.

title: Coherent optics move toward interoperability
summary: At OFC 2022, the OIF demonstrated significant advancements in optical networking, including interoperable coherent optics from various vendors, 112 Gb/sec electrical links, flexible Ethernet, and co-packaging techniques. These innovations address the increasing demand for higher data rates and improved efficiency in data centers and network infrastructure. Future developments include an 800G coherent optics spec and research into 224 Gb/sec electrical interfaces for 1.2 Tb/sec and 1.6 Tb/sec links.

title: Innovation or Intellectual Scam: Patent Damages and Their Exploited History
summary: The article discusses the high financial stakes and potential for exploitation within the patent law industry, focusing on the Caltech v. Apple and Broadcom case where Apple was relieved of a $1.1 billion patent infringement claim. It examines different interpretations of patent damages, the historical context of patent law, and the role of Patent-Assertion Entities (PAEs). The author also explores proposed reforms to patent law, such as viewing patents as a public regulatory system, to better promote innovation and standardize damage calculations.

title: Broadcom in talks to buy VMware
summary: Chipmaker Broadcom Inc. is reportedly in talks to acquire cloud service provider VMware Inc., according to sources familiar with the matter. This acquisition would further Broadcom's diversification into enterprise software, following previous purchases of CA Technologies and Symantec's security division. VMware's market capitalization was $40.3 billion as of Friday's close, with Michael Dell holding a 40% stake and Silver Lake owning 10%.

title: Broadcom in Talks to Acquire VMware, the Cloud Computing Company (Published 2022)
summary: Broadcom, a semiconductor giant, is reportedly in discussions to acquire VMware, a cloud computing company known for its virtualization software. This potential acquisition would expand Broadcom's presence in cloud computing and continue CEO Hock E. Tan's strategy of diversifying beyond chip companies following a blocked deal with Qualcomm in 2018. Following the news, VMware's stock saw a significant increase, while Broadcom's shares experienced a slight dip.

title: Chipmaker Broadcom in talks to acquire VMware for $60 billion -sources
summary: Broadcom Inc is reportedly in talks to acquire cloud service provider VMware Inc for $60 billion, a move that would significantly expand Broadcom's enterprise software business. The proposed deal, valued at around $140 per share in cash and stock for VMware, represents a 46% premium to VMware's Friday share price. This acquisition aligns with Broadcom CEO Hock Tan's strategy of diversifying beyond chips into infrastructure software.

title: Broadcom in talks to purchase cloud computing company VMware
summary: Chipmaker Broadcom is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire cloud computing company VMware in a cash-and-stock deal, aiming to further diversify its business into enterprise software. This potential acquisition follows Broadcom's previous purchases of CA Technologies and Symantec's security division. VMware's shares jumped on the news, while Broadcom's saw a slight drop, as details of the deal's value remain undisclosed.

title: Broadcom Inc in talks to acquire VMware: sources
summary: Broadcom Inc. is reportedly in discussions to acquire cloud-computing company VMware Inc., a deal that would significantly expand the chipmaker's software presence. This potential acquisition aligns with CEO Hock Tan's strategy of diversifying Broadcom through software companies. VMware, valued at around US$40 billion, would provide Broadcom with greater access to the data center market, a critical area for growth.

title: Broadcom to Buy VMware for $61 Billion in Record Chip Deal
summary: Broadcom Inc. has agreed to acquire cloud-computing company VMware Inc. for approximately $61 billion, marking one of the largest technology deals in history. This acquisition will strengthen Broadcom's software portfolio, a key part of CEO Hock Tan's strategy to diversify the chipmaker and expand its presence in corporate software. The deal, supported by major VMware shareholders, includes commitments for $32 billion in debt financing and is expected to close in Broadcom’s fiscal year 2023.

title: Broadcom buys VMware in a $61B acquisition deal
summary: Broadcom has formally announced plans to acquire VMware in a cash and stock deal valued at approximately $61 billion, plus an additional $8 billion in VMware net debt. The deal is expected to close in Broadcom's 2023 financial year, pending regulatory and shareholder approvals. This acquisition will see Broadcom's existing infrastructure and security software integrated into an expanded VMware portfolio, with the Broadcom Software Group rebranding to operate as VMware.

title: Broadcom to buy Palo Alto’s VMware for $61 billion
summary: Computer chip and software maker Broadcom is set to acquire Palo Alto-based cloud technology company VMware for approximately $61 billion, including $8 billion of VMware's net debt. This acquisition aims to strengthen Broadcom's position in the cloud computing market by integrating VMware's technology. The deal is one of the largest this year, highlighting a trend of significant tech buyouts amidst market uncertainty where tech companies are perceived to be undervalued.

title: Broadcom will tame the VMware beast
summary: Broadcom's planned acquisition of VMware for $61 billion signifies a new era for the virtualization leader, focusing on financial performance and shareholder value. The article details Broadcom's strategy of optimizing profitability by focusing on recurring revenue, cutting R&D and sales/marketing expenses for less profitable areas, and standardizing G&A. For customers, this likely means higher prices and a more focused product portfolio, with core offerings like vSphere being emphasized while less profitable or unproven ventures like Project Monterey face scrutiny.

title: Adas/Connected Car - June 2022 v1
summary: The "ADAS/Connected Car - June 2022" special report details advancements in automotive technology, focusing on advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) and the critical need for high-speed data transmission within vehicles. It addresses the challenges of integrating high-resolution cameras and radars, demanding greater bandwidth and lower latency in in-vehicle networks, and highlights the role of edge inference computers and radar technology in enhancing vehicle safety and performance. The report emphasizes the importance of testing solutions, processing capabilities, and continuous innovation in shaping the future of connected vehicles.

title: Deal Diary: Gibson, Wachtell Lead VMware Sale to Broadcom
summary: Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP advised VMware Inc. on its $61 billion sale to Broadcom Inc., with Broadcom also assuming $8 billion in debt. Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz, O’Melveny & Myers LLP, and Cleary Gottlieb Steen & Hamilton LLP counseled Broadcom. Various financial institutions provided advice to both parties, and Silver Lake Partners, a 10% owner of VMware, was represented by Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP, while Michael Dell (42% owner) was advised by Wachtell Lipton.

title: RSA 2022: The Road to Adversary Engagement
summary: At RSA 2022, experts discussed "adversary engagement," distinguishing between denial (stopping attacks) and deception (luring controlled intrusions to learn). This strategy helps organizations understand attacker TTPs and potentially identify adversaries. Openly discussing these tactics can deter criminals and foster community-wide information sharing, with FBI Special Agent Tony Rogers confirming its legality when conducted on an organization's own network.

title: Consumer groups urge shops not to partner with ‘predatory’ lender EasyPay
summary: A coalition of consumer advocacy groups is calling on auto repair shops, including large national chains and collision shops, to stop offering financing through EasyPay Finance and TAB Bank due to "predatory" lending practices. The groups highlight interest rates as high as 189%, hidden terms, and issues like unauthorized debits and damage to credit reports. They advise shops against partnering with lenders charging more than 36% APR and encourage consumers to be cautious and understand loan terms.

title: VMware Appoints Venkatesh Murali as General Manager and Country Leader, Nascent Markets and Vietnam
summary: VMware has appointed Venkatesh Murali as General Manager and Country Leader for Nascent Markets and Vietnam, leveraging his two decades of IT experience in solutions sales and channel development. He will focus on driving business strategy and growth by accelerating digital transformation in the region, particularly as developing economies in Asia and Vietnam's internet economy are projected for significant growth. Murali aims to help organizations in emerging Asian markets adopt hybrid and multi-cloud, modern applications, and cybersecurity solutions.

title: Broadcom's acquire-and-axe history concerns VMware users
summary: VMware users and employees are expressing concern over Broadcom's acquisition of the company, fearing a repeat of Broadcom's historical strategy of acquiring and then divesting parts of companies. Broadcom's CEO, Hock Tan, intends to transition VMware to a subscription-based model and integrate Broadcom's infrastructure and security software. The acquisition timing also coincides with a trend where corporate IT shops are moving towards SaaS-based solutions, diminishing VMware's traditional market.

title: Report: EU plans to investigate Broadcom’s proposed acquisition of VMware
summary: The European Union is preparing to launch a lengthy "phase 2" antitrust investigation into Broadcom's proposed $61 billion acquisition of VMware. This investigation could last over a year and potentially derail the deal, as multiple organizations, including VMware customer trade groups, have expressed concerns about potential price increases or forced bundling of Broadcom services. Broadcom is reportedly in preliminary discussions with EU officials to argue that the acquisition will not reduce competition or harm customers.

title: Chinese DPU chip firm gains funds, unicorn status ...
summary: Jaguar Microsystems, a Chinese Data Processing Unit (DPU) chip company founded in 2020 by former Broadcom executive Sunny Siu, has secured hundreds of millions of yuan in funding, propelling its valuation to unicorn status at approximately US$1.34 billion. The financing round was led by Tencent Holdings Ltd. and included other major investors. Jaguar Microsystems, which employs nearly 400 staff, released an FPGA version of its DPU in 2021 and plans for mass production of its system-chip version by 2023, primarily targeting cloud computing and internet companies, with Tencent expected to be a key customer.

title: Enabling Broadcom Db2 Tools users with DevOps Capabilities
summary: This article focuses on enabling Broadcom Db2 Tools users with DevOps capabilities. It discusses how organizations can leverage existing Db2 tools in a modern DevOps environment, enhancing efficiency and collaboration in database management.

title: Qualcomm’s new wi-fi 7 and RF front-end modules are gunning for 5G auto and IoT device markets
summary: Qualcomm Technologies has introduced new RF Front-End (RFFE) modules designed for Wi-Fi and Bluetooth, including Wi-Fi 7, extending its RFFE leadership beyond smartphones into automotive, XR, PCs, wearables, and IoT markets. These modules aim to help OEMs reduce development costs, accelerate commercialisation, and improve device performance and battery life. Industry experts like ABI Research suggest these innovations position Qualcomm to leverage its expertise in complex Wi-Fi technology and expand its market reach.

title: VMware Bolsters Southeast Asia and Korea Channel Strategy with Stephen Robinson as the Partner and Commercial Leader
summary: VMware has appointed Stephen Robinson as the Partner and Commercial Business Leader for Southeast Asia and Korea (SEAK). Robinson will focus on strengthening the company's partner strategy, channel programs, and driving digital transformation for commercial customers in the region. His appointment is expected to lead VMware's channel partner ecosystem to new levels of success in SEAK.

title: Direxion SOXL ETF: How To Trade This Leveraged Play On Semis (NYSEARCA:SOXL)
summary: This article discusses the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares ETF (SOXL), a 3X leveraged ETF on the semiconductor index. Despite SOXL being down 85% this year, the author advises against buying it immediately. The recommendation is to wait for signs of a bottom in the broader semiconductor index (SOXX) before initiating a position in SOXL.

title: Panasonic lawsuits say Broadcom violated computer tech patents
summary: Panasonic has filed lawsuits against Broadcom in Texas federal court, accusing the chipmaker of violating multiple computer-technology patents. Panasonic claims that Broadcom rejected offers to license its patent portfolio, which covers a wide range of Broadcom products including microprocessors and integrated circuits. The Japanese electronics giant is seeking unspecified monetary damages and a permanent injunction against Broadcom, alleging willful infringement given Broadcom had cited some of these patents in its own applications.

title: Tom Krause, Broadcom exec who helped lead VMware deal, leaving to be new Citrix CEO
summary: Tom Krause, who led Broadcom's software unit and was previously its CFO, is departing the company to become the new CEO of Citrix. Krause was a key figure in Broadcom's acquisition of VMware. His new role at Citrix has been confirmed, although public details about the move were initially withheld.

title: Broadcom loses top software exec in the middle of VMware deal to head combined Citrix-Tibco company
summary: Broadcom's head of software, Tom Krause, is departing the company to lead a combined Citrix-Tibco entity. This unexpected move comes amidst Broadcom's $61 billion acquisition of VMware, a deal Krause played a significant role in orchestrating, raising concerns among analysts. Krause's departure is effective Friday, and he will assume a new role at a privately held enterprise software company.

title: Hock Tan: Broadcom’s Plan For VMware As Krause Departs
summary: Broadcom CEO Hock Tan addressed VMware employees after the unexpected departure of software leader Tom Krause, who was originally slated to lead VMware post-acquisition. Tan announced he would personally oversee the integration efforts, ensuring VMware becomes part of the Broadcom Software Group, which he will now run. This comes as Broadcom proceeds with its $61 billion acquisition of VMware, which is expected to close by the end of its fiscal year 2023.

title: AIC J4078-02-04X 4U 78-Bay JBOD Enclosure With Broadcom 24Gb SAS Expanders
summary: AIC Inc. has launched the J4078-02-04X, a high-density 4U 78-bay JBOD enclosure supporting 24Gb SAS and Broadcom 24Gb SAS expanders. This enclosure offers significantly improved I/O performance compared to previous generations, supporting over 1.5PB of storage with 3.5" enterprise HDDs. Designed for high-density storage applications, it features dual-path redundancy, hot-swap components, and aims to provide a cost-effective solution for data-intensive workloads.

title: Key Takeaways: 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Security Service Edge
summary: This article provides key takeaways from the inaugural 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Security Service Edge (SSE), defining SSE and outlining Gartner's market predictions. It evaluates 11 significant providers, categorizing them into leaders, challengers, visionaries, and niche players based on their ability to execute and completeness of vision. The report highlights the strengths of companies like Zscaler, Netskope, McAfee Enterprise, Palo Alto Networks, Cisco, Bitglass, Lookout, Broadcom, iboss, Forcepoint, and Versa within the SSE market.

title: Nottingham City Council sells off contracting company behind Broad Marsh work
summary: Nottingham City Council has sold off Thomas Bow Ltd, a civil engineering and road surfacing contractor it owned for less than three years, stating it no longer had a "strategic purpose." The sale is part of a broader review of council-owned companies following the collapse of Robin Hood Energy and comes amid calls for financial transparency from opposition leaders who question the true cost and profitability of the company during council ownership. The council maintains the sale achieved "best value" and that services will continue through ongoing contracts.

title: MediaTek forms strategic partnership to make chips using Intel’s technology
summary: MediaTek Inc. has partnered with Intel Corp. to manufacture chips using Intel’s matured process technology, Intel 16 node, aiming to diversify its foundry sources beyond TSMC. This collaboration will enhance MediaTek's supply for mature process nodes used in devices like TVs and routers, while its partnership with TSMC for advanced nodes remains strong. Intel's Foundry Services will support MediaTek's smart edge devices, leveraging Intel's expanding manufacturing capacity in the US and Europe.

title: Arm: Business Strategy—SoftBank Group Report 2022
summary: This article details Arm's business strategy as presented in the SoftBank Group Report 2022. It covers Arm's role as a global leader in semiconductor technology, its licensing business model, and strategic investments in R&D to maintain market leadership and expand into new areas like autonomous vehicles and IoT. The report highlights significant growth in both technology royalty and non-royalty revenues in fiscal 2021 and discusses the leadership transition with Rene Haas appointed as CEO.

title: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) Gains As Market Dips: What You Should Know
summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) closed with a slight gain of 0.17% on a day when the broader market dipped. The chipmaker has outperformed the S&P 500 and the Computer and Technology sector over the past month. Analysts project significant year-over-year growth for Broadcom's upcoming earnings, with a positive outlook indicated by recent estimate revisions.

title: Global tech company Broadcom Inc. acquires Stillwater-based Hostbridge Technology
summary: Broadcom Inc., a global technology company, has acquired Stillwater-based Hostbridge Technology, a company specializing in integrating mainframe systems with newer applications. Hostbridge CEO Russ Teubner, who co-founded the company in 2000, announced the acquisition and plans to stay on, working from Stillwater. The move is expected to expand Hostbridge's reach in the market, though some sales and marketing positions will be cut as Broadcom has its own teams.

title: Expanding the Possibilities
summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) is challenging conventional semiconductor manufacturing by focusing on feature-rich process technology for 70% of the market. The company emphasizes innovation in RF communications, power management, and precision analog, working with industry leaders to deliver solutions across various high-growth markets. GF's efforts, including the GF Connex RF portfolio, GF Fotonix silicon photonics platform, and GF Labs, aim to drive product innovation and accelerate time-to-market.

title: Broadcom applies for consent order on unfair deals with Samsung
summary: US chipmaker Broadcom Inc. has applied for a consent order from the Korea Fair Trade Commission (KFTC) following allegations that it forced Samsung Electronics Co. and other Korean tech firms into long-term contracts. The KFTC will decide whether to approve the consent order, which would involve Broadcom compensating the affected Korean firms and would conclude the investigation. The allegations stem from Broadcom allegedly compelling smartphone and set-top box manufacturers to exclusively use its communication chips, banning the use of competitors' products for three years.

title: Are Semiconductors Set to Soar?
summary: The semiconductor sector, a leading indicator for the broader stock market, has experienced a significant correction in 2022 due to supply chain constraints and an eventual potential supply glut. However, the recent passage of the CHIPS Act, which subsidizes U.S.-made semiconductor chips, could provide a significant boost to the sector. Direxion offers leveraged ETFs like SOXS for bearish outlooks and SOXL for bullish outlooks, tracking 300% of the daily inverse or performance of the ICE Semiconductor Index, respectively.

title: As the electric scooter market booms, Razer shifts gears with the NIU X Razer SQi Edition
summary: Razer has partnered with NIU to release a limited-edition electric scooter, the NIU X Razer SQi Edition, which sold out in just two seconds in China. The e-scooter features Razer's signature green accents, an aerospace-grade magnesium alloy chassis, smart capabilities like Siri control and NFC ignition, and advanced security features. Priced at approximately $1,500 USD, it offers a 25km/h top speed and a range of 65km.

title: What has Broadcom become?
summary: Broadcom, once a semiconductor leader, has transformed into a private equity-like entity focused on acquiring companies with strong market positions, slashing costs, and driving cash flow through consolidation. This strategy, initiated by CEO Hock Tan, has led to significant stock growth but raises concerns about reduced R&D and the long-term sustainability of the roll-up model, especially as the company increasingly targets software acquisitions. The article suggests that while the model has been highly successful for shareholders, its future depends on continued acquisition opportunities and the energy of its executive leadership.

title: Compare Cortex XDR vs. VMware Carbon Black
summary: This article compares Palo Alto Networks Cortex XDR with VMware Carbon Black, highlighting Cortex XDR's superior performance in threat prevention, detection, and investigation based on independent third-party evaluations like MITRE ATT&CK and AV-Comparatives EPR. It emphasizes Cortex XDR's comprehensive capabilities across various data sources and automated incident response, while raising concerns about Carbon Black's reliance on third-party prevention, limited visibility, manual investigation processes, and potential uncertainties following Broadcom's acquisition of VMware.

title: VMware Blockchain is Bringing Ethereum to the Enterprise
summary: VMware is launching VMware Blockchain for Ethereum in beta to address critical gaps in current Ethereum platforms for enterprise use cases, such as privacy, scalability, and governance. This new offering builds on VMware's existing enterprise blockchain platform and provides an EVM-compatible solution with high throughput, ZKP-based privacy capabilities, and enterprise-grade operations. The Bank of Israel and Infosys are early adopters, with the Bank of Israel using it for its Digital Shekel Project and Infosys developing a Vital Records Management Solution.

title: VMware CEO weighs in on Broadcom acquisition, multicloud and why supercloud is the future
summary: VMware CEO Raghu Raghuram discussed the planned Broadcom acquisition, his company's multicloud strategy, and the evolution toward supercloud, emphasizing that Broadcom sees VMware as a valuable asset rather than just a numbers-driven deal. He highlighted VMware's early bet on multicloud starting in 2018 and the introduction of VMware Aria, a true multicloud management solution that federates data sources in real-time using GraphQL. Raghuram stressed that Aria differentiates itself by pulling data directly across various clouds, rather than acting as an interface for separate products.

title: VMware Embraces Kubernetes APIs in VMware vSphere 8
summary: VMware has integrated Kubernetes APIs into its vSphere 8 platform, aiming to simplify the management of virtual machines and Kubernetes clusters for DevOps teams. This update, part of VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid 2.0, seeks to reduce the number of APIs required and make Kubernetes environments more accessible to IT administrators. The company is also supporting DPUs and enhanced GPU capabilities to boost performance for AI models.

title: Flat Panel Antenna Market to Accrue $5.1 Billion, Globally, By 2031 at 28.1% CAGR: Allied Market Research
summary: The global flat panel antenna market is projected to reach $5.1 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 28.1% from 2022 to 2031, according to Allied Market Research. This growth is driven by the extensive use of flat-panel antennas in maritime satellite communications, space exploration, military, and commercial aircraft radars, and the naval sector. While a lack of experienced workforce and high maintenance costs could hinder growth, increasing applications across various end-use verticals are expected to open new opportunities.

title: Skyworks (SWKS) Unveils Network Synchronization Solutions
summary: Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) has launched new network synchronization solutions, including Si551x and Si540x products and AccuTime IEEE 1588 software, to support 5G fronthaul networks with precise time synchronization. These solutions, developed in partnership with NXP Semiconductors and Advanced Micro Devices, are crucial for emerging 5G applications and the IoT. Despite a challenging macroeconomic environment and competition, Skyworks aims to leverage organic growth and an expanding product portfolio to capitalize on 5G and IoT megatrends.

title: Broadcom not to force S.Korean tech companies on deals
summary: Broadcom has pledged to stop pressuring South Korean tech companies like Samsung Electronics into unfair deals, seeking a consent order from the Korea Fair Trade Commission to avoid penalties. The chipmaker allegedly compelled Samsung to purchase $760 million worth of components and will now propose corrective measures, including setting up funds for SMEs. The FTC will review Broadcom's proposal and collect opinions before making a final decision.

title: Iridium announces their 9th SpaceX launch to occur in mid-2023
summary: Iridium Communications Inc. has agreed with SpaceX for a ninth launch, Iridium-9, in mid-2023 from Vandenberg Space Force Base. This mission will deploy up to five of Iridium's ground spare satellites from the Iridium NEXT program via a Falcon 9 rocket. The company reports continued growth in its customer base and expanded product offerings since completing its upgraded constellation in 2019.

title: MaxLinear 'AnyWAN' chips target FTTP, DOCSIS and FWA
summary: MaxLinear has introduced a new family of AnyWAN broadband gateway chipsets designed to support multiple access networks and software platforms, including FTTP, DOCSIS, and FWA. These chips aim to simplify operations for device manufacturers and operators, while also helping to alleviate supply chain constraints. The AnyWAN family comprises three products, with volume production expected in Q4 2022, and MaxLinear is also working on DOCSIS 4.0 integration for future roadmap developments.

title: Small Business UCaaS M&A: Ooma Acquires OnSip -
summary: Ooma has acquired OnSip, a unified communications service provider specializing in small businesses, for $9.75 million in cash. This acquisition aims to expand Ooma's leadership in serving SMB customers and accelerate growth for Ooma Business. The article also provides context on the broader UCaaS market, noting some signs of consolidation and slowing M&A activity in 2022.

title: Webworm: Espionage Attackers Testing and Using Older Modified RATs
summary: Symantec, by Broadcom Software, has identified a group named Webworm actively testing and using customized versions of older Remote Access Trojans (RATs) like Trochilus, Gh0st RAT, and 9002 RAT for espionage. This group, likely linked to "Space Pirates," targets government agencies and IT service providers primarily in Asian countries. Their methodology involves using modified backdoors and evasive techniques, with observed activities indicating pre-deployment or testing stages of these retooled malware variants.

title: Broadcom switch SDK software enables rapid development and deployment
summary: Broadcom's switch Software Development Kit (SDK) is designed to accelerate the development and deployment of network solutions. This SDK provides tools and interfaces that streamline the process for developers working with Broadcom switch hardware. It aims to reduce time to market for new networking products.

title: Lumentum and Coherent carve up VCSEL market
summary: Lumentum and Coherent now dominate the market for VCSELs, collectively holding 80% market share according to a Yole Intelligence report. Their dominance is largely attributed to close ties with Apple and strategic acquisitions, as VCSEL technology, initially expected to proliferate widely, has primarily seen sustained adoption in Apple iPhones. The VCSEL market is projected to reach $4 billion by 2027, driven by datacom and growing automotive applications.

title: Corporate Tax Rate Report
summary: WalletHub.com's latest Corporate Tax Rate Report analyzes the 2021 federal, state, and international tax rates of S&P 100 companies, revealing an average overall tax rate of 18%, two percentage points lower than in 2020. The report highlights companies paying the highest and lowest taxes, including several with negative tax rates, and features expert commentary on the U.S. corporate tax structure, its complexity, and its impact on revenue and individuals.

title: Apple Begins Making iPhone 14 in India Weeks Ahead of Schedule
summary: Apple has started manufacturing its new iPhone 14 in India earlier than expected, significantly reducing the production lag between China and India from months to mere weeks. This move aligns with Apple's strategy to diversify its supply chain amidst geopolitical tensions and disruptions in China, while also supporting India's ambition to become a major technology manufacturing hub. The accelerated timeline is a result of resolving supply chain issues and collaboration with manufacturing partner Foxconn.

title: "It's good the stupid stuff on Wall Street has disappeared"
summary: Len Rosen, an investment banker with Evercore, discusses the recent hostile takeover attempt of Ceragon Networks and predicts an increase in similar situations due to depressed market caps. He notes that while the IPO market is currently closed, M&A activity is expected to rise, particularly for companies valued up to $1.5 billion, and highlights the strength and maturity of the Israeli tech sector despite economic slowdowns. Rosen also reflects on past market bubbles and the current macroeconomic environment's impact on investment banking.

title: How Juniper Networks is innovating networks with Broadcom
summary: This article discusses how Juniper Networks is innovating its networking solutions, particularly with technologies from Broadcom. The collaboration aims to enhance network performance and capabilities to meet evolving industry demands. The focus is on leveraging advanced hardware and software to drive the next generation of networking infrastructure.

title: How Juniper Networks is Innovating Networks with Broadcom
summary: This article highlights Juniper Networks' strategic partnership with Broadcom, focusing on how this collaboration drives innovation in networking. Juniper's CEO, Rami Rahim, discusses the company's shift towards "Experience-First Networking" to simplify complex technological challenges and the ongoing push for innovation through AI, automation, and machine learning to make networks seamless and less visible to the user. The partnership with Broadcom is crucial for providing the advanced semiconductor solutions needed to achieve these goals.

title: Lumen gives MediaTek subsidiary Airoha a fiber foothold in the US
summary: Lumen Technologies has partnered with MediaTek subsidiary Airoha Technology to provide chips for Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) supporting its 8 Gbps fiber service tier. This collaboration marks Airoha's U.S. debut and extends a previous two-year partnership with Lumen. The move is significant for Airoha as it establishes its first North American market presence and could also increase competition for dominant chip supplier Broadcom.

title: FTC ‘Second Request’ Investigation Of Broadcom-VMware Deal Launches: 5 Things To Know
summary: The Federal Trade Commission has initiated a "second request" investigation into Broadcom's proposed $61 billion acquisition of VMware, a regulatory step that halts the deal until the review is complete. Broadcom stated this is common for a transaction of this magnitude and expressed confidence in obtaining regulatory approval. The company and VMware intend to cooperate fully, with a shareholder vote on the merger scheduled for November 4th.

title: VMware Report Exposes Emotet Malware’s Supply Chain
summary: A new report from VMware's Threat Analysis Unit (TAU) details the reemergence and evolving tactics of the Emotet malware. After a coordinated takedown, Emotet has reappeared as a sophisticated malware-as-a-service, constantly shifting its infrastructure and evolving its attack patterns to evade detection. The report provides insights into Emotet's execution chains, new modules for stealing credit card information and lateral movement, and methods for obfuscating its command and control infrastructure, helping organizations strengthen their defenses.

title: At Broadcom, innovation never stops
summary: Broadcom's commitment to continuous innovation is a core aspect of its operational philosophy. The company consistently emphasizes the ongoing development and introduction of new technologies and solutions.

title: Microsoft Teams SIP Gateway to support analog phones via Analog...
summary: Microsoft announced at Ignite that its Teams SIP Gateway will support analog phones starting in early 2023, allowing existing analog endpoints like door access systems and elevator phones to connect to Teams. It will support specific Analog Telephone Adaptors (ATAs) from AudioCodes, Cisco, and Poly. Additionally, SIP Gateway will support Algo overhead paging devices, ringers, and speakers, also coming in 2023.

title: Columbia Research Enhanced Core ETF (RECS) and Columbia Research Enhanced Value ETF (REVS) Celebrate Three-Year Milestone
summary: Columbia Threadneedle Investments announced the three-year anniversary of its Columbia Research Enhanced Core ETF (RECS) and Columbia Research Enhanced Value ETF (REVS). These strategic beta ETFs, launched in 2019, leverage quantitative investment research and active insights, offering a cost-effective and tax-efficient vehicle for passive benchmark investors. Both funds have shown strong performance, with RECS achieving a 5-star Overall Morningstar Rating and REVS a 3-star rating, affirming their objective of enhancing market exposure within passive investments.

title: Charter changes approach for its cable access network – sources
summary: Charter Communications is shifting its distributed access architecture strategy for its hybrid fiber/coax (HFC) network, now favoring remote PHY (R-PHY) with a virtual cable modem termination system (CMTS) over remote MACPHY. This change, which follows an in-depth review, aligns Charter more closely with broader industry trends and aims to create greater economies of scale, particularly benefiting from Comcast's existing R-PHY deployments. While Charter's primary focus for DOCSIS 4.0 remains Extended Spectrum DOCSIS (ESD), the move to R-PHY and vCMTS could open new opportunities for chipmakers and various network equipment vendors.

title: Silicon Labs CEO Transforms Company to IoT Chip Designer
summary: Silicon Labs CEO Matt Johnson is transforming the company into a "pure-play" IoT chip designer, focusing solely on wireless connectivity and IoT devices. The company aims to become the undisputed leader in this space, having doubled its revenue in two years and being an early adopter in unifying various IoT standards like Matter, Zigbee, and Zwave. Despite facing competition from larger players and a recent softening of demand, Johnson sees significant growth potential, particularly in areas like digital retail labels and secure hardware.

title: Chart of the Week: Advisors Shifting Focus on Dividend ETFs
summary: Demand for dividend ETFs was high in 2022 due to rising interest rates,, but advisor focus might be shifting from high-yielding funds to those emphasizing capital appreciation and total return, particularly index-based dividend growth ETFs or actively managed dividend ETFs. This shift is influenced by rising 10-year Treasury yields exceeding 4.0% and advisors prioritizing capital appreciation over pure income.

title: Symantec researchers find new hacking tools used in stealthy ‘Cranefly’ campaign
summary: Symantec researchers have discovered new hacking tools and techniques used in a stealthy campaign by a threat actor dubbed "Cranefly" (UNC3524). This group employs a dropper named Trojan.Geppei to install previously unknown malware like Denfuan, which uniquely reads commands from Internet Information Services logs. Cranefly is characterized by its long dwell times on victim networks and its methods for avoiding detection, with the likely motivation being intelligence gathering.

title: Cubic SiC: Tomorrow’s champion for power electronics?
summary: This article explores the potential of cubic Silicon Carbide (3C-SiC) as a reliable alternative to 4H-SiC and GaN in power electronics, particularly in electric vehicles. While 4H-SiC devices face reliability issues related to gate oxides, 3C-SiC offers advantages in processing, interface quality, and enhanced reliability due to its intrinsic properties and higher band offset. The author discusses ongoing research at Swansea University that highlights 3C-SiC's superior channel mobilities and gate oxide stability, positioning it as a promising material for future automotive power devices despite its early developmental stage.

title: Lidar duo Ouster and Velodyne to merge in all-stock deal
summary: Lidar companies Ouster Inc and Velodyne Lidar Inc have agreed to an all-share merger to consolidate their operations and pursue profitability in the autonomous vehicle technology sector. Both companies have seen significant stock value decreases due to extended timelines for autonomous vehicle deployment. The merger, which will result in a combined market capitalization of approximately $400 million, aims for at least $75 million in annualized cost savings within nine months.

title: HPE SVP Hang Tan On HPE, VMware ‘Co-Innovation,’ And The $32.3B Private Cloud Opportunity
summary: HPE Senior Vice President Hang Tan discusses the new HPE GreenLake for VMware offering, emphasizing the 'massive' private cloud opportunity for partners. This collaboration integrates VMware Cloud Foundation with HPE GreenLake's consumption-based model, simplifying hybrid cloud deployments for enterprise customers. Tan highlights benefits like true consumption-based pricing, fully integrated experiences, co-innovation, and reduced complexity for channel partners, positioning HPE as a leader in the growing $32.3 billion private cloud market.

title: The real winners of the chip wars are in East Asia
summary: The US is expanding restrictions on China's access to semiconductor technology, leading to a surge of investment in semiconductor production facilities across East Asia as supply chains shift away from China. Countries like Malaysia and Japan are set to benefit significantly from this redirection of investment, while South Korea and Singapore navigate a more complex path, balancing trade relations with both the US and China. Vietnam is also emerging as a major winner, attracting substantial foreign investment in its semiconductor industry.

title: Global financial services CIO conversation: Transforming the partnership with Broadcom's acquisition of VMware
summary: This article discusses the transformation of partnerships within global financial services due to Broadcom's acquisition of VMware. It focuses on the conversation among CIOs regarding this significant industry change.

title: Buffett’s Taiwan Semiconductor Stake Highlights Beaten-Down Semi Stocks
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has invested $4.1 billion in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, drawing attention to the semiconductor industry, which Morningstar analysts consider largely undervalued. Following a significant selloff in 2022 due to recession fears and U.S. restrictions on sales to China, many semiconductor companies, including Skyworks Solutions, Teradyne, and Taiwan Semiconductor, are rated as significantly undervalued. The article details Morningstar's perspectives on Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, ASML, Broadcom, and Texas Instruments, highlighting investment opportunities despite industry challenges.

title: Multiple firms prepare for wave of lawsuits over Huawei SEPs
summary: Huawei has initiated a wave of lawsuits in Germany against several companies, including Stellantis, Amazon, Netgear, and AVM, regarding its mobile phone and Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents (SEPs). This marks a shift for Huawei from being predominantly a defendant to an aggressor in patent litigation. The cases, which are being handled by various law firms, highlight the increasing importance and contentious nature of SEPs in connected car and wireless technologies.

title: AMD chip to power auto supplier Aisin’s new automated car parking system
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) announced that its Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC microprocessor will power a new automated parking system developed by Japan's Aisin Corp. This system aims to make automated parking more mainstream by offering a cost-effective solution for automakers. The use of AMD's FPGA-based chip, acquired from Xilinx, will allow for post-sale software and chip updates in vehicles, with production expected to begin on 2024 models.

title: Broadcom’s latest switch chip offers connectivity alternative for hyperscalers
summary: Broadcom's new Tomahawk 5 switch processor aims to provide a connectivity alternative for hyperscalers by using Remote Direct Memory Access over Converged Ethernet (RoCE) to offload data movement from CPUs. This technology, along with the new Atlas2 switch based on PCIe Gen 5, targets the need for lower processing costs and higher performance in data-intensive applications. The company highlights significant savings by having the switch handle data transfer directly between servers, bypassing CPU involvement.

title: Astera Labs Accelerates Company Growth with Series-D Funding and Expanded Board of Directors
summary: Astera Labs announced significant company growth, including closing a $150M Series-D funding round led by Fidelity Management & Research, which raised its valuation to $3.15B. The company also expanded its Board of Directors with Dr. Alexis Black Bjorlin of Meta and Michael Hurlston of Synaptics, and grew its product portfolio with the Leo Memory Connectivity Platform for CXL™. These milestones, alongside global expansion with new R&D centers in Canada, position Astera Labs for continued leadership in connectivity solutions for intelligent systems.

title: Ethernet Switch and Router Market Size to Grow by USD 6074.87 million From 2022 to 2027, Assessment on Parent Market, Five Forces Analysis, Market Dynamics & Segmentation - Technavio
summary: The global Ethernet Switch and Router Market is projected to grow by USD 6074.87 million from 2022 to 2027, with a CAGR of 2.67% and a Y-o-Y growth rate of 1.61%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing demand for colocation data centers among SMEs. North America is expected to contribute 42% to this growth, largely due to the adoption of data centers by various service providers and governments. The market will also see trends like the rising use of open Ethernet switches, despite challenges posed by interoperability issues.

title: Broadcom's $61 bln deal for VMware on UK regulator's radar
summary: The UK's competition regulator is scrutinizing Broadcom Inc.'s $61 billion proposed acquisition of VMware Inc. The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is concerned the deal could substantially lessen competition and is seeking public comments by December 6 to aid its assessment. This acquisition, announced in May, marks Broadcom's expansion into enterprise software and is the second-largest deal globally this year.

title: Sony and Lexar-trusted encryption provider leaked sensitive data for over a year
summary: ENC Security, a Netherlands-based software company renowned for providing encryption solutions for storage devices from brands like Sony and Lexar, had been leaking critical business data for over a year due to a misconfiguration by a third-party supplier. The leaked data included sensitive credentials and API keys, creating a "goldmine" for threat actors and raising concerns about potential phishing campaigns and ransomware attacks. Although ENC Security claims the issue is resolved and affected a small percentage of users with no signs of malicious activity, the incident highlights the risks associated with relying on "military-grade" security claims without thorough scrutiny.

title: Hock Tan | Broadcom and VMware: Investing for customer value
summary: This article, titled "Hock Tan | Broadcom and VMware: Investing for customer value," suggests a strategic focus from Broadcom and VMware on enhancing customer value. The title implies a business-oriented perspective from Hock Tan regarding the direction and investment strategies for the combined entity.

title: Nutanix buyout may be on the cards with HPE sniffing around
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is reportedly considering acquiring hyperconverged infrastructure vendor Nutanix, with discussions ongoing for several months. The potential acquisition aligns with HPE's GreenLake everything-as-a-service (XaaS) platform strategy, aiming to enhance its software offerings and attract more customers. Nutanix's recent financial recovery and improved market cap make it an attractive target, especially given HPE's struggles in expanding the GreenLake model to its global partners.

title: Broadcom switches provide unrivaled flexibility for copper and optical connectivity
summary: The article states that Broadcom switches offer unparalleled flexibility for both copper and optical connectivity solutions. This suggests that their products support a wide range of networking requirements.

title: AVGO Earnings: Highlights of Broadcom’s Q4 2022 report
summary: Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) reported better-than-expected earnings and revenues for Q4 2022, with adjusted net profit of $10.45 per share and revenues increasing 21% annually to $8.93 billion. The company announced plans to resume its $13 billion share repurchase program and increased its quarterly dividend by 12% to $4.60 per share for fiscal year 2023. This performance indicates strong financial growth and a commitment to returning value to shareholders.

title: Broadcom gives upbeat revenue forecast as server, data center demand booms
summary: Broadcom Inc. projected first-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates, indicating strong demand for its chips used in data centers and networking equipment. The company's focus on infrastructure for hybrid work models gives it an advantage over competitors. Broadcom also expects to benefit from the global 5G rollout and its venture into software.

title: New business to bring more jobs to Plainview
summary: A new joint venture called DSM Semichem LLC. is set to begin construction on a facility in Plainview, Texas, in January, which will produce electronic sulfuric acid (ELSA) and create new jobs. This development is a result of Samsung's investment in a Texas facility and its need for local resources. The Plainview location will be the first U.S. facility for Dongjin Semichem Co., Ltd., and its operations are anticipated to begin after January 1, with full operation planned by the end of 2024.

title: Broadcom identifies the 2023 tech trends that will transform IT
summary: This article outlines Broadcom's predictions for the key technology trends expected to transform IT in 2023. It highlights the company's insights into the evolving landscape of information technology.

title: Fiber Optic Component Market Size, Share & Growth | 2031
summary: The global fiber optic component market was valued at $23.6 billion in 2021 and is projected to reach $53.6 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 8.8%. This growth is driven by increasing data center deployments, internet penetration, demand for bandwidth, and advancements in IoT. The market is segmented by type (cables, amplifiers, connectors, transceivers, etc.), data rate, and application with communications and cables dominating the market.

title: HPE Says ‘There Are No Discussions With Nutanix’
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has denied any ongoing discussions to acquire Nutanix, following a report from Dealreporter that talks had ceased. This statement caused Nutanix shares to drop. Earlier reports from Bloomberg had indicated that HPE had previously expressed takeover interest in Nutanix, leading to a temporary rise in Nutanix's market capitalization.

title: Applied Materials Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Shares of Applied Materials Inc. dropped 1.17% to $94.23 on Wednesday, marking its fourth consecutive day of losses, despite outperforming the broader S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high, although it performed better than several key competitors like Broadcom, Qualcomm, and Advanced Micro Devices. Trading volume was also below its 50-day average.

title: boAt Nirvana Eutopia with Spatial Audio, Nirvana 525 ANC and more to be showcased at CES 2023
summary: boAt is set to showcase its latest hearable products and technologies developed by 'boAt Labs' at CES 2023. These innovations include the Nirvana Eutopia headphones with Spatial Audio and head-tracking capabilities, the Nirvana 525 ANC neckband with Personalized Adaptive EQ, and other products co-engineered with global tech companies like Dolby, Dirac, Mimi, and CEVA, aiming to provide an immersive and premium audio experience. The company emphasizes its commitment to developing cutting-edge technology in India for a global audience.

title: MOSFET Relay Market - Global Growth Drivers & Opportunities
