summary: The MOSFET relay market is projected to grow from USD 237 million in 2022 to USD 474 million by 2030, driven by increasing demand for instrumentation devices, high-tech electronics adoption, automation in consumer electronics, and complexity in EV circuits. Asia Pacific is expected to be the largest market due to the growth in automotive, test & measurement, and medical industries in emerging economies. Key players include TE Connectivity, Standex Electronics, Panasonic Corporation, Schneider Electric, and Omron Corporation.

title: Semtech Corporation and Sierra Wireless Announce Termination of the Waiting Period Under the HSR Act
summary: Semtech Corporation and Sierra Wireless announced the termination of the waiting period under the Hart-Scott-Rodino Antitrust Improvements Act of 1976, clearing a regulatory hurdle for Semtech's acquisition of Sierra Wireless. The companies now expect the closing of the transaction to occur on or about January 12, 2023, subject to remaining closing conditions. This development follows a Second Request for information from the U.S. Department of Justice regarding the acquisition.

title: Qualcomm Introduces Snapdragon Satellite, The World's First Satellite-Based Solution Capable of Supporting Two-Way Messaging for Premium Smartphones and Beyond
summary: Qualcomm announced Snapdragon Satellite, the world's first satellite-based two-way messaging solution for premium smartphones, enabling global connectivity starting with devices based on the Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 Mobile Platform. This solution, powered by Snapdragon 5G Modem-RF Systems and supported by the Iridium satellite constellation, will offer emergency messaging and general two-way communication. Qualcomm plans to expand Snapdragon Satellite beyond smartphones to other devices, with Garmin collaborating to support emergency response services.

title: Inari’s share price down over 4% as Apple plans to drop Broadcom chip from devices
summary: Inari Amertron Bhd's share price dropped over 4% after reports that Apple plans to replace Broadcom's Wi-Fi and Bluetooth chips with in-house designs by 2025. Although Inari's exposure to Broadcom is mainly on complex RF chips, which are harder to replace in the short term, the news still impacted the company's stock given its significant reliance on Broadcom. Apple is Broadcom's largest customer, accounting for about 20% of its revenue, and Inari is heavily dependent on Broadcom.

title: Knee-jerk reaction on Inari shares as Apple reportedly plans to drop Broadcom chip from devices
summary: Inari Amertron Bhd's stock price fell significantly following news that Apple plans to drop Broadcom's Wi-Fi and Bluetooth chips, though analysts believe Inari, which focuses more on RF chips, will see a muted impact. Despite its strong reliance on Broadcom and Apple, the company's long-term fundamentals remain intact, although concerns persist about a broader replacement of Broadcom chips and the current semiconductor oversupply.

title: Apple to start using in-house screens from 2024 - Bloomberg News
summary: Apple plans to begin using its own custom displays in mobile devices starting in 2024, aiming to reduce reliance on external suppliers like Samsung and LG. The transition will start with high-end Apple Watches, followed by other devices including the iPhone, and will involve upgrading to microLED technology. This move is part of a broader strategy by Apple to bring more component production in-house, following similar shifts with chips from Broadcom and Intel.

title: Inari pares loss as analysts see limited impact from Apple's plan to drop Broadcom chip
summary: Inari Amertron Bhd shares recovered some losses after analysts indicated that Apple's plan to replace Broadcom chips with in-house designs would have a limited impact on Inari's outsourced semiconductor assembly and test (OSAT) business. Analysts noted Inari's direct exposure to Apple's revenue through Broadcom is relatively small (17-18%) and that Inari's involvement in complex RF filters is unlikely to be immediately affected. Despite a maintained "market perform" rating with a slightly reduced target price, some analysts suggest the negative news is already priced in, presenting a potential buying opportunity.

title: BCV: Signs Of Stabilizing With An Attractive Discount (NYSE:BCV)
summary: The article discusses how Bancroft Fund (BCV) has shown signs of stabilization following a period significantly impacted by interest rate fluctuations. With interest rate hikes potentially nearing an end, the fund's stability is expected to continue. BCV is highlighted as offering an attractive discount and diversified exposure to convertible bonds for investors.

title: Back Again At Comparing Skyworks Vs. Qorvo (NASDAQ:SWKS)
summary: This article compares Skyworks Solutions and Qorvo, both RF suppliers currently trading at low P/E ratios. It highlights Qorvo's transparency regarding market details and Skyworks' stable growth due to its strong ties with Apple. The author also suggests Qorvo has a significant opportunity with Apple's business, especially given Qualcomm's apparent withdrawal.

title: Taiwan's TSMC ranked No. 1 on China's top 500 company list
summary: The Shanghai-based Hurun Research Institute controversially ranked Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) as No. 1 on its Hurun China 500 list of most valuable private companies in China, surpassing Tencent Holdings. This ranking led to widespread mockery from Taiwanese netizens who pointed out the absurdity of a Taiwanese company being listed as "China's No. 1." The report also incorrectly described TSMC's headquarters as "Hsinchu, Taiwan, China."

title: UK competition watchdog probes $61 bln Broadcom-VMware deal
summary: Britain's Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has initiated an investigation into Broadcom Inc's $61 billion acquisition of cloud-computing firm VMware Inc. This move reflects global regulatory scrutiny over large tech deals, prompted by concerns about potential market dominance and impact on competition. The CMA's investigation is in its first phase, with a decision expected by March 22.

title: GNSS Simulators Market Size growing at 10.9% CAGR Set to Reach USD 430.3 Million By 2023
summary: The global GNSS Simulators Market is projected to reach USD 430.3 Million by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 10.9% from USD 171.2 Million in 2021. This growth is driven by increasing demand from the aerospace and defense, automotive, and geospatial sectors, as well as advancements in GNSS simulation technologies. North America is expected to hold the largest market share due to significant R&D investments and the presence of major players.

title: Fortinet Continues to Invest in Custom Chips to Power Security Offerings
summary: Fortinet continues to invest in its custom ASIC chip strategy, which executives claim is a unique differentiator and a key factor in the vendor's firewall market dominance. The company designs its own Arm-based ASICs for its products to achieve better performance, efficiency, and cost savings compared to traditional CPU-based solutions. This long-term investment also provides supply chain advantages and contributes to Fortinet's leading market share in firewall appliance unit shipments.

title: VMware drops on report FTC talking to third parties in Broadcom deal review
summary: VMWare's stock dropped following a report that the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) is consulting with industry players regarding Broadcom's proposed acquisition of VMware. The FTC is reportedly seeking input from various entities as part of its review process for the deal. This news likely caused investor concern about the acquisition's approval, leading to the decline in VMware's stock price.

title: Here's A Simple Way To Bet On Semiconductor Plays Like NVDA Stock
summary: The semiconductor sector is experiencing a resurgence fueled by a new Nasdaq "power trend," offering investors opportunities in stocks like Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor, Advanced Micro Devices, and Broadcom. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) provides a way to capitalize on these rallies. The article highlights the SMH ETF as a simple investment vehicle for those looking to profit from the recovery and growth in semiconductor stocks.

title: Petrobras Taps Maersk, Alcatel for Mero Field PRM System
summary: Petrobras has awarded Alcatel Submarine Networks and Maersk Supply Service a contract to supply and install a Permanent Reservoir Monitoring (PRM) system for the Mero field in Brazil. The system, involving 400 kilometers of optical fibers, will monitor and optimize oil production from the deep offshore pre-salt area. Installation is slated to begin in 2024 to enhance reservoir management and increase oil recovery.

title: Home Security System Market is Booming Worldwide | Honeywell International, Vivint, MOBOTIX
summary: The "Home Security System Market - Global Outlook and Forecast 2022-2028" report by HTF Market Intelligence Consulting Pvt. Ltd. evaluates the market, identifying key trends, growth drivers, technologies, and investment structures. It profiles major players like Honeywell International, Vivint, and MOBOTIX, and segments the market by product type (Video Surveillance, Alarm Systems, Access Control) and end-users (Individual Users & Building Contractors), providing a global and regional analysis. The study aims to offer insights for strategic and tactical decision-making within the home security system industry.

title: Pushing the System-in-Package Concept Into the Future
summary: Chipletz, a fabless substrate vendor, has selected Siemens semiconductor packaging technologies for their Smart Substrate based advanced packages. This technology aims to revolutionize semiconductor in-package functionality, bridging the gap between Moore's Law slowing and the rising demand for compute performance for critical AI workloads and high-performance computing. Chipletz's unique approach enables the integration of multiple ICs from different manufacturers within a single package, supported by Siemens' EDA tools.

title: Arrow Electronics picks up two Global Broadcom Awards
summary: Arrow Electronics was honored with two Global Broadcom Partner awards at the Broadcom Partner Momentum Summit in Barcelona: "Cybersecurity Aggregator of the Year" and "Technical Support Provider of the Year." These awards recognize Arrow's significant contributions to Broadcom's commercial cybersecurity segment in Europe, including launching a new partner program and investing in technical support capabilities. The summit highlighted the strong collaboration between Arrow and Broadcom in driving innovation and success for partners and customers.

title: Ethernet Switch Market Size, Share, Trends, Analysis, Growth Rate, and Forecast 2029
summary: The Ethernet Switch Market was valued at US$ 5.38 billion in 2021 and is projected to reach US$ 7.40 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 4.07%. The market's expansion is primarily driven by increased Gigabit Ethernet usage, the adoption of Power over Ethernet (PoE), the convergence of residential and business networks, and the rising demand for internet-connected devices. Key growth segments include fixed configuration Ethernet switches and the data center sector, with significant contributions from SMEs and cloud adoption in regions like India.

title: Major Semiconductor Firm Applied Materials Hit by Supply Chain Attack; Ransomware Impact Will Cost $250 Million
summary: A supply chain attack on a business partner of semiconductor giant Applied Materials is projected to cost the company $250 million in the upcoming quarter. While the specific partner was not named, MKS Instruments, an industrial equipment supplier that recently announced a ransomware attack, is a likely candidate. The incident highlights the growing vulnerability of the semiconductor industry to cyberattacks and the widespread consequences that can arise from supply chain compromises.

title: iShares U.S. Tech Independence Focused ETF Holdings List (BATS:IETC)
summary: This article provides a comprehensive list of the top holdings for the iShares U.S. Tech Independence Focused ETF (IETC) as of February 28, 2023. It details the company, current price, weight, and shares held for each of the top 25 companies, alongside breakdowns of geographic, currency, sector, industry, and sub-industry exposures. The ETF is heavily weighted towards technology, software, and semiconductor companies.

title: NatWest Group Optimizes User Experience Through Improved Security and Development Environments
summary: NatWest Group has improved its user experience by enhancing security and development environments, leveraging VMware solutions. The company upgraded to VMware Aria Automation 8, which introduced GitOps integration, infrastructure as code, and automated testing, significantly freeing up developers' time and improving code quality. Additionally, NatWest is progressing towards a Zero Trust security model, focusing on engaging its teams to ensure a secure-by-default stance for applications.

title: Web3: Is your Enterprise Ready for the Decentralized Future?
summary: The article discusses Web3, the next iteration of the internet characterized by decentralization, peer-to-peer transactions, and user control over data and identity. It explains how Web3, based on blockchain technology and smart contracts, differs from Web2 and highlights its potential to disrupt various industries. The piece also outlines key Web3 use cases such as Decentralized Finance (DeFi), central bank digital currencies, digital payments, and Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAOs), urging enterprises to invest in robust blockchain platforms and integrate Web3 into their digital strategies to avoid being left behind.

title: SparkFun Things Plus Matter Connects DIY IoT Devices to Home Automation
summary: SparkFun has released the Thing Plus Matter board, designed to accelerate IoT and smart home device development. It features a Silicon Labs MGM240P Multi-protocol Wireless Module with an Arm Cortex-M33 core and supports Matter for integrating various home automation ecosystems like Apple HomeKit, Amazon Alexa, and Google Assistant. The board, compatible with the Feather form factor, also includes SparkFun's Qwiic Connect System and is supported by Silicon Labs' Simplicity Studio IDE.

title: VMware’s SaaS Sales Surge As Broadcom Deal Nears
summary: VMware reported a 4 percent increase in sales for the last fiscal year, with Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) and subscription annual recurring revenue growing by 30 percent to $4.6 billion. The company anticipates its merger with Broadcom, which aims to boost subscription revenue, will close in Broadcom's current fiscal year. VMware also announced a new partnership with HPE GreenLake to offer an integrated hybrid cloud solution.

title: MWC: KT (Korea) partners with Intel to become first Wi-Fi 7 operator, Qualcomm claims 175 Wi-Fi 7 design wins
summary: During Mobile World Congress, Korea Telecom (KT) announced a partnership with Intel to develop and promote Wi-Fi 7 services, aiming to be the world's first operator to deploy the standard. Simultaneously, Qualcomm revealed it has secured 175 design wins for new Wi-Fi 7-capable access points and end-user devices, showcasing its strong position in the nascent Wi-Fi 7 market. Both companies are moving forward with Wi-Fi 7 technology, with Intel expected to release chipsets later this year and Qualcomm already shipping numerous Wi-Fi products.

title: STMicroelectronics’ all-in-one motion and bone-conduction sensor saves space and power in hearables and headsets
summary: STMicroelectronics has released the LSM6DSV16BX, a highly integrated all-in-one motion and bone-conduction sensor designed for hearable devices like true wireless stereo (TWS) headphones and AR/VR/MR headsets. This sensor combines a 6-axis IMU, an audio accelerometer for bone-conduction voice detection, and ST’s Qvar™ technology for touch controls. It leverages Sensor Fusion Low Power (SFLP) technology and in-edge processing resources to significantly reduce system power consumption and PCB area, while improving performance and user experience in ear-worn devices.

title: China’s chip sector needs more than state funding
summary: China plans to invest US$140 billion into its chip sector to overcome US export curbs, but industry experts suggest that state funding alone is insufficient. The country needs to foster innovation and break free from emulating existing foreign technology to move up the value chain, as current practices leave Chinese firms at a disadvantage compared to Western rivals. Collaboration between equipment manufacturers and clients, similar to that seen in the aviation industry, is crucial for technological advancement and problem-solving, which Chinese companies currently lack due to isolation and sanctions.

title: Buy Nvidia (NVDA) & Broadcom (AVGO) Stock for More Upside?
summary: This article from March 2023 analyzes whether investors should buy Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) stock, given their significant year-to-date gains. It examines their momentum, valuation, and growth prospects, concluding that while both are "Hold" rated by Zacks, their strong historical performance and continued relevance in the semiconductor market suggest they could still be rewarding holdings despite potential short-term pullbacks.

title: NETGEAR Introduces the First WiFi 7 Router, Unlocking the Next Generation of High-Performance Connectivity
summary: NETGEAR has launched the Nighthawk RS700, the industry's first WiFi 7 router, designed to meet the increasing demand for high-performance connectivity in modern homes. This new router offers speeds up to 19Gbps, supports up to 200 concurrent devices, and features a sleek design with enhanced antenna technology for broad coverage. It also includes advanced security with NETGEAR Armor and multi-gigabit ports to support current and future internet speeds.

title: Coding for the future of U.S. national defense

He highlights the role of multi-cloud strategies and software factories, enabled by Tanzu, in developing cloud-native applications for the Department of Defense. These initiatives aim to modernize IT infrastructure, accelerate innovation, and build self-sustaining software capabilities within various branches of the U.S. military.

Driven by massive data, a skilled workforce, supportive government policies, and an advanced manufacturing base, Chinese companies like Baidu, ByteDance, Alibaba, and Tencent are poised to become leaders in generative AI. The article details the foundational strengths of China's AI ecosystem and the potential beneficiaries within its tech and semiconductor industries.

title: Building A Broader IT Modernization Strategy

summary: The Nonvolatile Random Access Memory (NVRAM) market is projected to reach USD 3.06 Billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 13.5% from 2022. title: The top 10 biggest technology companies in the US in 2023
summary: This article lists the top 10 biggest technology companies in the US in 2023 by market capitalization. The list includes giants like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, highlighting their significant contributions and innovations in the technology sector.

title: Kodiak Robotics, Forward Air unite for Atlanta-Dallas AV route
summary: Kodiak Robotics and Forward Air are collaborating on an autonomous vehicle (AV) freight route between Atlanta and Dallas, operating 24 hours a day, six days a week, with a safety driver team. Since August, a Kodiak truck has completed over 100 loads and driven more than 100,000 miles for Forward Air. While Forward Air's CEO believes AV trucks won't replace independent contractors, he sees potential for a scalable solution on certain lanes, especially for expedited and time-sensitive freight.

title: iShares MSCI USA Momentum Factor ETF (MTUM) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the iShares MSCI USA Momentum Factor ETF (MTUM), including its current stock price, holdings, and dividend yield. The article also provides key statistics, performance data, and historical breakdowns of its holdings and stock picks.

title: Renesas Cellular-to-Cloud Development Kits Now Connect Seamlessly to Microsoft Azure Cloud Services
summary: Renesas Electronics Corporation announced that its cellular-to-cloud development kits, the CK-RA6M5 and CK-RX65N, now fully support Microsoft Azure cloud services. These Azure-certified kits, featuring 32-bit MCUs and an LTE CAT-M1 module, enable reliable and secure end-to-end cloud solutions for IoT devices. The integration includes Microsoft's new Embedded Wireless Framework and enhanced security measures, simplifying the connection and management of wireless IoT devices.

title: 2 Under-The-Radar Small Chip Stocks Poised For Big Growth
summary: This article highlights two under-the-radar fabless semiconductor companies, Impinj Inc. (NASDAQ: PI) and Allegro MicroSystems, Inc. (NASDAQ: ALGM), that are showing strong growth potential despite being smaller players in the chip industry. Both companies are experiencing increasing sales and earnings, with analysts expecting continued growth in the coming years. The article details their specific market niches and financial performance, positioning them as attractive opportunities for investors.

title: 5G technology market size to grow by $128.46 bn till 2026
summary: The 5G technology market is projected to grow by $128.46 billion, accelerating at a CAGR of 69.34% between 2021 and 2026, primarily driven by increasing R&D and deployment of 5G networks. Significant investments and innovations from major vendors like Huawei, Cisco, CommScope, and Nokia are key contributors. Governments worldwide are also supporting this growth through efforts to roll out 5G infrastructure.

summary: Sony Semiconductor Solutions Corporation (SSS) has made a strategic minority investment in Raspberry Pi Ltd. (RPL) to enhance the development of edge AI solutions. This partnership aims to integrate SSS's AITRIOS™ platform, which supports edge AI devices, into the Raspberry Pi ecosystem, making advanced machine-learning applications accessible to a wider community. The collaboration builds on an existing relationship between the two companies in semiconductor products and manufacturing.

title: Top Circuit Protection Kits Manufacturers in the World
summary: This article identifies and describes leading global manufacturers of circuit protection kits. These kits are crucial for safeguarding electronic circuits from various conditions like transient voltages, inrush current, and overheating. The article highlights key players such as Littelfuse, Bourns, Ametherm, Eaton, and TDK, detailing their contributions and solutions in the circuit protection industry.

summary: This Zacks Analyst Blog highlights five high-growth tech stocks—Analog Devices, Broadcom, Adobe, Asure Software, and MSCI—to consider buying as inflation shows signs of cooling. These companies are focused on innovation, efficiency, and sustainability to cater to diverse applications across various industries.

title: Supreme Court Rules District Courts May Consider Structural Challenges to SEC and FTC Administrative Processes
summary: The Supreme Court unanimously ruled in Axon Enterprise, Inc. v. Federal Trade Commission that constitutional challenges to the SEC and FTC administrative processes can be heard directly in federal district courts. This decision means plaintiffs do not need to exhaust these arguments through agency enforcement processes first, which is expected to encourage more constitutional challenges to agency enforcement schemes in federal courts. The ruling clarifies the jurisdictional question but does not address the underlying constitutional challenges to the agencies' structures.

title: DENSO and UMC Subsidiary USJC Collaborate on Automotive IGBT Production
summary: DENSO and United Semiconductor Japan Co. Ltd (USJC), a subsidiary of UMC, have commenced mass production of insulated gate bipolar transistors (IGBTs) for automotive applications at USJC's 300mm fab in Japan. This collaboration, which started with a strategic partnership announcement a year ago, aims to produce a new generation of IGBTs with 20% reduced power losses for electric vehicles. Production is projected to reach 10,000 wafers per month by 2025, supporting the accelerating adoption of EVs.

title: EU Gets Commitments Offer in Broadcom-VMware Merger Review
summary: Broadcom Inc. has offered antitrust commitments to the European Commission regarding its proposed $61 billion acquisition of VMware Inc. The European regulator subsequently extended its deadline to rule on the deal to July 17, as it reviews the potential impact of the merger. Details of the commitments offered by Broadcom were not immediately disclosed.

title: Apple announces multibillion-dollar deal with Broadcom for components made in the USA
summary: Apple has announced a new multiyear, multibillion-dollar agreement with Broadcom to develop 5G radio frequency and wireless connectivity components. These components, including FBAR filters, will be designed and built in the USA, notably in Fort Collins, Colorado. This investment aligns with Apple's 2021 commitment to invest $430 billion in the U.S. economy, supporting American manufacturing and job growth while expanding 5G technology.

title: Apple inks multi-billion-dollar deal with Broadcom for U.S.-made chips
summary: Apple has announced a multi-billion-dollar deal with Broadcom to source U.S.-made chips. Under the multi-year agreement, Broadcom will develop 5G radio frequency components, including FBAR chips, at facilities in the U.S., including Fort Collins, Colorado. This move signifies Apple's continued efforts to diversify its supply chains and invest in domestic manufacturing.

title: Cadence Cerebrus garners Singapore Business Review's tech award for AI-semiconductor services

This technology automates and scales digital chip design, optimizing power, performance, and area (PPA) while significantly reducing engineering effort and time to tape out. The Cadence Cerebrus has been widely adopted by top semiconductor companies and hyperscalers, revolutionizing chip design productivity.

title: Markets Brief: Why Vanguard Sees a Brighter Outlook for Investors’ Portfolios
summary: Vanguard Group forecasts a brighter outlook for investors, primarily due to higher bond yields that now offer meaningful contributions to portfolios. While stock valuations remain stretched in the U.S., increasing interest rates have made fixed-income investments more attractive, particularly government bonds. Vanguard's long-term forecasts suggest improved returns for both bonds and stocks compared to recent years, highlighting a more balanced investment landscape.

title: GPUs get all the headlines, but the future of AI is real-time data
summary: This article discusses the burgeoning AI market, emphasizing that while GPUs steal the spotlight, the true future of AI lies in real-time data and automation. It highlights Nvidia's dominance in the AI hardware space and the competitive landscape with other chipmakers and hyperscalers, while also examining the challenges and shifts in data platforms like Snowflake and Databricks. The author argues that real-time data will drive massive productivity growth, transforming current data architectures and application development.

title: Business in Asia-Pacific: A Year of Learning and Adapting

It discusses the region's advanced digital adoption and leadership in multi-cloud strategies compared to other global markets. The author emphasizes the dynamic nature of APJ, with examples of companies leveraging cloud technology for innovation and sustainability.

title: Frontgrade and Lattice Develop Low SWAP-C FPGA
summary: Frontgrade Technologies and Lattice Semiconductor have collaborated to develop the Certus-NX-RT FPGA, a radiation-tolerant, low-power field-programmable gate array designed for space and satellite applications. Built on Lattice’s Nexus platform, the Certus-NX-RT offers significantly reduced size, weight, power, and cost (SWAP-C) compared to similar devices, providing up to four times lower power consumption. This development addresses the critical needs of the aerospace and defense industries for demanding space applications like distributed computing and platform management.

title: SCHD: Put The Power Of Compounding Into Overdrive
summary: The article discusses the Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD) as a strong investment choice for building passive income through compounding dividends. It highlights SCHD's potential for growth, solid yield, and strong dividend growth, positioning it as a diversified and low-cost option for long-term investors seeking both yield and dividend growth. The author, Mark Roussin, emphasizes the "life-changing money" that can be generated over time through the power of compounding with SCHD.

title: High Beta ETF 'SPHB' Lifted by Heavy Tech Sector Tilt

This growth is driven by the increasing demand for wireless connectivity, the proliferation of IoT devices, and the global expansion of 5G networks. Key market players include IQE PLC, NXP Semiconductors N.V., and TDK Corporation, with the Asia Pacific and North America regions expected to see significant growth.

title: Broadcom and Google Cloud Announce License Portability Support for VMware Cloud Foundation on Google Cloud VMware Engine
summary: Broadcom and Google Cloud have announced support for license portability of VMware Cloud Foundation to Google Cloud VMware Engine, allowing customers to use their VMware Cloud Foundation subscriptions flexibly across Google Cloud and on-prem data centers. This initiative aims to provide greater flexibility, investment protection, and lower total cost of ownership for customers, enabling them to seamlessly migrate workloads and leverage integrated Google Cloud services. The license portability is expected to be publicly available in the second quarter of calendar year 2024.

title: George Soros’ fund bets on U.S. leisure travel, with fresh stakes in JetBlue, Spirit, Sun Country
summary: Billionaire investor George Soros's fund, Soros Fund Management, has expanded its portfolio in the U.S. leisure travel sector, initiating new positions in low-cost airlines JetBlue, Spirit, and Sun Country. This move indicates a bullish outlook on budget air travel, while the fund simultaneously divested from technology companies Arm Holdings and Broadcom.

title: Who is Hock Tan, Broadcom CEO and new Meta Board Member?
summary: Hock Tan, CEO and President of Broadcom, has been appointed to Meta's board of directors, a move Mark Zuckerberg believes will aid in Meta's Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) goals. Tan, known for his strategic acquisitions and transformation of Broadcom into a tech heavyweight, will bring extensive industry expertise to Meta. His career highlights include leading Broadcom through significant growth, including the recent $61 billion acquisition of VMware.

title: 25 Most Valuable Semiconductor Companies In The World
summary: The semiconductor industry faced a 9.4% sales decline in 2023 but is projected to rebound in 2024, exceeding 2022 record revenues. Key players like NVIDIA, TSMC, and Broadcom are highlighted, with NVIDIA focusing on AI infrastructure, TSMC expanding manufacturing in Japan, and Broadcom reporting strong Q4 2023 earnings. The article lists the 25 most valuable semiconductor companies globally, ranked by market capitalization as of February 15, 2024.

title: After Earnings, Is Arista Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar maintains that Arista Networks' stock is overvalued despite strong fourth-quarter earnings and a bullish long-term forecast driven by high-speed cloud providers and AI investments. While the company is positioned for significant market share gains, its current valuation is considered overexuberant. Morningstar assigns Arista a "wide moat" rating due to its intangible assets and customer switching costs in high-speed networking, but also a "High Uncertainty Rating" due to customer concentration and challenges in expanding into new market segments.

title: SPY ETF Reaches Record High Amid Nvidia-Fueled AI Rally—Key Chart Pattern to Watch
summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) reached a record high, mirroring the S&P 500's surge driven by mega-cap technology stocks, particularly after Nvidia's strong earnings report. Nvidia, along with other AI-related companies like Microsoft and Broadcom, contributed significantly to the rally, highlighting the outsized influence of Magnificent Seven stocks within the SPY's portfolio. Investors should watch the SPY's rising wedge pattern for potential breakouts or breakdowns, which could signal future price movements.

title: Broadcom to offload VMware’s remote access computing business to KKR in $3.8B deal
summary: Broadcom is selling VMware Inc.'s End User Compute (EUC) business to private equity firm KKR & Co. Inc. in a $4 billion deal. This move is part of Broadcom CEO Hock Tan's strategy to streamline business operations after the $69 billion acquisition of VMware in November. The EUC business, which sells software for remote desktop and application access, is expected to complement KKR's existing virtualization software company, Alludo.

title: Broadcom CEO’s Pay Soars to $161.8 Million After Stock Award
summary: Broadcom CEO Hock Tan's annual compensation more than doubled to $161.8 million in the past year, largely due to a $160.5 million stock award that will vest over five years. While his salary remained $1.2 million, this stock award led to a 167% increase in his total compensation. He did not receive non-equity incentive compensation, which he had received the previous year.

title: Broadcom CEO’s Pay Soars to $161.8 Million After Stock Award
summary: Broadcom Inc. CEO Hock Tan's annual compensation more than doubled in 2023, reaching $161.8 million. This significant increase was primarily due to a "front-loaded" stock award of $160.5 million, which will vest over five years. His base salary remained at $1.2 million, and he did not receive non-equity incentive compensation this past year.

title: Broadcom CEO compensation more than doubles to $161.8 mln in 2023
summary: Broadcom CEO Hock Tan's annual compensation surged to $161.8 million in 2023, more than double his 2022 pay of $60.6 million. This increase was largely driven by $160.5 million in stock awards, positioning his pay at 510 times the median employee salary. The chipmaker's financial success follows its $69 billion acquisition of VMware and a significant rise in its stock value.

title: KKR To Acquire Broadcom’s End-User Computing Division for $4 Billion
summary: Global investment firm KKR plans to acquire Broadcom Inc.'s End-User Computing Division for approximately $4 billion. This acquisition will transition the division into an independent company, aiming to enhance digital workspace solutions globally with a focus on growth through R&D and strategic partnerships. The EUC Division, previously part of VMware, offers key products like Horizon and Workspace ONE.

title: AI and silicon: Shaping future tech innovation
summary: Broadcom's President, Charlie Kawwas, discusses the company's strategy in integrating AI and silicon technology to drive innovation and ensure sustainability. He highlights the critical role of silicon in enabling AI workloads, emphasizing the need for robust network and connectivity strategies. Broadcom's approach focuses on openness, scalability, and power efficiency, including collaboration with VMware to enhance software-hardware integration.

title: Best Long-Term Semiconductor Stocks According To Redditors: Favorites Beyond Nvidia
summary: This article explores Reddit's discussions on the best long-term semiconductor stocks, moving beyond Nvidia to consider other significant players. Recommendations include diversified ETFs like VanEck Vectors Semiconductor ETF (SMH), foundational manufacturers like ASML and Applied Materials, upcoming challengers such as AMD, and growth opportunities with Broadcom and TSMC. The community emphasizes the importance of thorough research and personal financial goals in investment decisions within the semiconductor sector.

title: As CHIPS Act Money Is Set to Be Distributed, Experts See Pivotal Point in Establishing U.S. Market Structure for the Long Term, Creating High Stakes for Intel, TSMC, Broadcom, Qualcomm, Nvidia, Micron, and Texas Instruments
summary: The article discusses the significant impact of the CHIPS Act funding distribution on the U.S. semiconductor market, with experts viewing it as a pivotal moment for long-term market structure. The Department of Commerce has recently disbursed $1.5 billion to GlobalFoundries Inc (GFS), and further billions are expected for other domestic manufacturers, notably Intel (INTC). This development is seen as having high stakes for major companies like TSMC, Broadcom, Qualcomm, Nvidia, Micron, and Texas Instruments.

title: Broadcom 2023 Partner Awards
summary: Broadcom announced its 2023 Partner Awards, recognizing key partners for their outstanding accomplishments in driving innovation, market execution, enablement investment, and technical expertise. The awards highlight partners who delivered superior customer outcomes across various categories including Global Winners, Expert Advantage, Mainframe, Cybersecurity, Enterprise Software, and VMware. The award recipients include numerous companies like Rego, CDW, HCLTech, Insight, and many others, showcasing their contributions to Broadcom's success in different regions and solutions.

title: So verdienst du mit Buyback-ETFs bei Aktienrückkäufen mit
summary: This article explains how investors can profit from share buybacks through Buyback-ETFs. It highlights that companies systematically repurchasing their own shares tend to perform better in the long run, and details two specific ETFs (Amundi S&P 500 Buyback ETF and Invesco Global Buyback Achievers UCITS ETF) that enable targeted investment in such companies, either globally or focusing on US titles. The article also discusses the advantages and disadvantages of share buybacks and identifies major companies utilizing this strategy.

title: MGV: Large Value Makes More Sense Now
summary: The article suggests a potential sector rotation as momentum favors large caps, but growth large caps show signs of fatigue. It highlights the Vanguard Mega Cap Value ETF (MGV) as a suitable investment for those bullish on large-cap value, noting its diversified portfolio including companies like Berkshire Hathaway and Exxon Mobil. The author, Michael A. Gayed, CFA, discusses his investment philosophy focused on risk-on/risk-off signals.

title: Governor Abbott Announces Appointments To Texas Semiconductor Innovation Consortium Executive Committee
summary: Governor Greg Abbott announced the inaugural members of the Texas Semiconductor Innovation Consortium Executive Committee (TSIC EC) at the University of Texas Dallas. The TSIC EC will guide the consortium's efforts and collaborate with industry experts to establish Texas as a national leader in advanced semiconductor research, design, and manufacturing. These appointments follow the Texas CHIPS Act, signed by Governor Abbott, which aims to solidify Texas's leading position in the semiconductor industry both nationally and globally.

title: Charles Clough Jr. acquires $100,200 worth of Clough Global Opportunities Fund shares
summary: Charles Clough Jr., an individual affiliated with the investment advisor to Clough Global Opportunities Fund (NYSEAMERICAN:GLO), purchased 20,000 common shares totaling $100,200. This transaction increases his total holdings to 300,444.8229 shares, signaling potential confidence in the diversified global investment fund. InvestingPro highlights include a significant 11.57% dividend yield and low price volatility, despite the fund not being profitable over the last twelve months.

title: Intel awarded up to $19.5 billion in grant, loan for US chip production
summary: The Biden administration has awarded Intel Corp. a federal grant of up to $8.5 billion and a loan of up to $11 billion to expand semiconductor manufacturing in Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio, and Oregon. This is the largest award from the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to reduce U.S. dependence on foreign chip suppliers. The funding is expected to stimulate over $100 billion in private investment from Intel and create 10,000 manufacturing jobs and 20,000 construction jobs.

title: Lilly CEO reaffirms commitment to Chinese market amid US threat against local biotechs
summary: Eli Lilly's CEO, David Ricks, reconfirmed the company's dedication to the Chinese market during a meeting with China’s Commerce Minister Wang Wentao, expressing willingness to increase investment and R&D cooperation despite heightened U.S.-China geopolitical tensions. This commitment comes as Lilly has several drug applications under review in China and amid concerns about a U.S. bill, the BIOSECURE Act, that could impact partners like WuXi AppTec. The move distinguishes Lilly from some U.S. peers, aligning more with European pharmaceutical companies that have recently made significant commitments to China.

title: Broadcom Inc. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors despite daily gains
summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) saw its stock rise by 0.41% to $1,353.47 on Friday, marking its fifth consecutive day of gains. Despite this increase, the stock underperformed compared to the broader market, as the S&P 500 Index fell by 0.14% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell by 0.77% during the same trading session.

title: Should You Invest in the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ)?
summary: The Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) offers diversified and low-cost exposure to the Technology - Semiconductors sector, tracking the PHLX Semiconductor Sector Index. With $359.59 million in assets and a 0.19% expense ratio, it has shown strong performance, gaining 17.37% year-to-date and 57.63% in the last year as of March 26, 2024. The ETF holds a Zacks ETF Rank of 1 (Strong Buy), making it a recommended option for investors.

title: Sidus Space Reports Full Year 2023 Financial Results and Provides Business Update
summary: Sidus Space (NASDAQ:SIDU) announced its full year 2023 financial results, highlighted by a significant improvement in gross margin to 28% and the successful launch of its first LizzieSat™ satellite. The company reported a total revenue of $6.0 million for 2023 and, subsequent to year-end, raised $15.2 million in gross proceeds to support its constellation expansion. Sidus Space emphasizes its transition towards high-margin, recurring revenue streams from satellite data and continues to advance its 3D-printed, AI-enhanced satellite technology.

title: Keysight Bids £1.2 Billion for Spirent, Overtaking Viavi Offer
summary: Keysight Technologies has made an offer to acquire UK telecom network testing company Spirent Communications Plc for £1.16 billion ($1.5 billion), surpassing an earlier bid from Viavi Solutions Inc. The offer values Spirent shares at 201.5 pence, including a 2.5 pence special dividend, leading Spirent's board to withdraw its recommendation for Viavi's £1 billion proposal.

title: Drone Co. Taking on China Sees Big Opportunities in US, Ukraine
summary: Unusual Machines Inc. (UMAC) aims to challenge China's dominance in drone parts production, leveraging new U.S. federal laws and global demand, particularly from Ukraine. Their strategy focuses on establishing an American supply chain for drone components, especially motors, to capitalize on the National Defense Authorization Act which restricts the use of Chinese-made drone technology by the U.S. government and its contractors. This move is expected to create significant opportunities in both the commercial and military drone markets, as Ukraine seeks diversified suppliers amid its conflict.

title: VMware says Tanzu is key to strategy after Broadcom acquisition
summary: VMware is seeking to reassure customers that its Tanzu application development and modernization toolkit is central to its post-Broadcom acquisition strategy. Purnima Padmanabhan, general manager of the Tanzu division, stated that Tanzu is one of four key VMware operating units and its development will focus on simplifying the platform, improving application management, and driving the adoption of the Spring framework. The company also aims to enable multicloud deployments and integrate AI services, positioning Spring as a crucial tool for developers building AI-powered applications.

title: Cashing in on the Bitcoin Bounce: 3 Stocks to Buy for Indirect Crypto Exposure
summary: The article identifies three stocks—MercadoLibre (MELI), Broadcom (AVGO), and SBI Holdings Inc (SBHGF)—that offer investors indirect exposure to the cryptocurrency market. These companies are involved in various blockchain-related activities, such as crypto trading, integration of blockchain technology, and investments in crypto startups, providing an alternative for those hesitant about direct crypto investments. The author suggests these stocks allow investors to benefit from Bitcoin's performance while diversifying their portfolios and maintaining traditional stock stability.

title: Broadcom and Google Cloud Announce Expanded Partnership to Help Accelerate Innovation for Enterprises
summary: Broadcom and Google Cloud have expanded their partnership to accelerate enterprise innovation. This collaboration includes optimizing Broadcom's VMware workloads for Google Cloud, joint go-to-market initiatives, and integrating Google Cloud's generative AI capabilities into Broadcom's offerings. Additionally, the partnership aims to simplify cloud deployments for customers and support the portability of VMware Cloud Foundation licenses to Google Cloud VMware Engine.

title: 20 Chip Manufacturing Companies in India to Know
summary: This article highlights 20 key chip manufacturing companies in India, emphasizing the country's growing importance in the semiconductor industry across design, fabrication, and materials development. It details how the rise of AI has heightened the demand for advanced chipsets, positioning India as a crucial hub for global semiconductor firms. The listed companies include major players like NVIDIA, AMD, Samsung, and Intel, alongside specialized Indian firms contributing to various aspects of semiconductor technology.

title: Ceva Wireless Platform IP Family Accelerates Enhanced Connectivity in MCUs and SOCs
summary: Ceva Inc. has launched Ceva-Waves Links, a new family of multi-protocol wireless platform IPs designed to meet the growing demand for connectivity-rich chips in smart edge devices across various markets. These IPs integrate standards like Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, UWB, and IEEE 802.15.4, offering optimized, easy-to-integrate communication subsystems. ABI Research projects significant growth in Wi-Fi plus Bluetooth combo chipset shipments, highlighting the industry's shift towards on-die chip integration for multiple connectivity protocols.

title: Qualcomm-built advanced video processor by Teledyne FLIR powers AI at the edge
summary: Teledyne FLIR has launched the Teledyne FLIR AVP, an advanced video processor featuring the Qualcomm QCS8550 chip, designed to power its Prism AI and computational imaging at the edge. This small, lightweight, and low-power module offers best-in-class AI performance for integration into various unmanned systems and security applications. The AVP runs Teledyne FLIR's Prism AI and ISP software libraries, offering advanced perception and image processing capabilities, and is expected to accelerate AI workloads up to five times faster than competitors.

title: Semiconductor Market size is set to grow by USD 156.39 billion from 2023-2027, Analog Devices Inc., Broadcom Inc. and Infineon Technologies AG, and more to emerge as Some of the Key Vendors, Technavio
summary: The global semiconductor market is projected to grow by USD 156.39 billion from 2023 to 2027, with a CAGR of 5.08%, driven by increasing technology integration, renewable energy, and IoT. Key vendors such as Analog Devices Inc., Broadcom Inc., and Infineon Technologies AG are capitalizing on this growth through strategic alliances and partnerships. APAC is expected to hold the largest market share, experiencing significant incremental growth during the forecast period.

title: USMV: The Right Time But Be Careful With Tech Exposure
summary: The iShares MSCI USA Min Vol Factor ETF (USMV) is a low-volatility ETF designed to offer less risk exposure in U.S. equities, focusing on diversification across sectors with Information Technology as its largest holding. The author, Michael A. Gayed, CFA, suggests that playing defense by lowering volatility is crucial, regardless of whether one is bearish or long, aligning with the fund's objective. He cautions about the ETF's tech exposure despite its overall low-volatility strategy.

title: Top 10 Smartwatch Companies | By Best Technology
summary: The article provides an overview of the smartwatch market, highlighting key drivers such as wearable technology adoption and technological advancements, and forecasting the market size to reach USD 138.7 Billion by 2033. It then details the top ten companies in the smartwatch market, including Apple, Samsung, and Google, offering company overviews, key management, financials, geographical presence, and recent developments for each. The report underscores the continuous innovation and strategic expansions by these major players in the growing wearable technology sector.

title: IonQ Appoints Peter Chapman as Chairman, Harry You as Lead Director in Drive For Quantum Market
summary: IonQ, a leader in quantum computing, has announced the appointment of Peter Chapman as Chairman of the Board and Harry You as Lead Independent Director, effective June 5, 2024. Chapman, also the CEO, has driven IonQ's transition from an academic to a commercial entity, achieving significant milestones. You brings extensive experience in generating shareholder value and will continue as Chairman of the Audit Committee, bolstering IonQ's leadership in the growing quantum computing market.

title: Alphabet's First Dividend: What You Need to Know
summary: Alphabet announced its first-ever quarterly dividend of US$0.20 per share, following a similar move by Meta Platforms. This initial dividend will result in a modest yield of approximately 0.5% for Alphabet shares, significantly less than its historical buyback yield. While the new dividend rate represents only 12% of morningstar equity analyst's earnings per share forecast, leaving substantial room for future increases, share repurchases are expected to outweigh dividends in the near term.

title: Super Micro suffers drop following outlook, AI-related stocks follow suit: update
summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) experienced a 15% drop in mid-afternoon trading, dragging down other AI and computer hardware-related stocks. Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Micro Technology (MU) also saw declines. This slump followed an updated outlook for Super Micro, impacting the broader AI sector.

title: Broadcom Drives Business Simplification, Portfolio Innovation, and Ecosystem Standardization for VMware Cloud Foundation Business
summary: Broadcom announced significant updates to its VMware software portfolio and the Broadcom Advantage Partner Program, focusing on business simplification, portfolio innovation, and ecosystem standardization for VMware Cloud Foundation. Key changes include a transition to subscription licensing and per-core pricing, a streamlined product portfolio, and enhanced partner profitability through a redefined VCSP program. These strategic shifts aim to deliver greater value to customers, foster continuous innovation, and optimize the private and hybrid cloud experience.

title: Alchip says revenue to grow in 2026
summary: AI chip designer Alchip Technologies Ltd expects significant revenue growth in 2026, driven by a new major AI chip customer, despite a stock price drop and increasing competition. The company emphasizes its strong partnerships with cloud service providers and its unique capabilities in leading-edge process technologies (5nm, 4nm, 3nm) for ASIC design. Alchip also reported a 104% increase in net profit for the last quarter and is prioritizing advanced packaging capacity from TSMC to meet AI chip production demands.

title: N8 Identity Named Broadcom IDM Partner of the Year for North America
summary: N8 Identity has been awarded Broadcom IDM Partner of the Year for North America for 2023, recognizing its two decades of commitment to delivering innovative identity management solutions. This award highlights N8 Identity's dedication to customer success and strong partnership with Broadcom, aiming to enhance security and streamline operations for organizations. Shawn Upson, VP of Sales, emphasized the team's hard work and collaboration in empowering clients with cutting-edge identity management.

title: Can Crowding Scores Quantify US Stocks’ Fragility?
summary: The U.S. equity market has reached an unprecedented level of concentration, largely driven by mega-cap tech stocks, sparking concerns about market fragility and crowding risk. While crowded stocks globally have historically underperformed uncrowded ones, the U.S. market has recently bucked this trend, with crowded stocks outperforming since mid-2022. The MSCI Security Crowding Model offers insights into identifying areas of the market most susceptible to price correction if sentiment deteriorates.

title: VMware settles patent lawsuit after Densify's $84.5 mln trial win
summary: Broadcom's VMware has settled a patent infringement lawsuit with rival software company Densify after a jury awarded Densify $84.5 million last year. Both companies specialize in cloud-computing software, and Densify had accused VMware of infringing patents related to optimizing "virtual machines." This settlement follows a previous $237 million verdict for Densify in 2020 that was later overturned.

title: GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. Reports First Quarter 2024 Financial Results
summary: GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. reported its Q1 2024 financial results, showing a 7% revenue increase year-over-year to $3.3 million and an income from operations of $7.2 million, largely due to a gain from a terminated development agreement with Samsung. The company is advancing its 5G chipset development, expects broad sampling by Q4 2024, and foresees volume shipments in H1 2025. GCT also announced a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Aramco to develop the 4G and 5G ecosystem in Saudi Arabia and secured an equity line of credit for up to $50 million to fund operations.

title: Wall Street Favorites: 3 ETFs With Strong Buy Ratings for May 2024
summary: This article highlights three ETFs that Wall Street analysts have given strong buy ratings for May 2024. These include the Vanguard Growth Index Fund ETF (VUG), iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), and iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF), all of which have demonstrated strong performance and offer exposure to growth stocks and key sectors like technology and semiconductors. The funds aim to provide investors with diversification and impressive returns, with a particular focus on the "Magnificent Seven" companies.

title: EMS Market Set for Rapid Growth Amid Tech Advances
summary: The Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) market is projected to grow significantly from $476.88 billion in 2023 to $701.47 billion by 2030, driven by advancements in technology and increasing demand across various sectors like consumer electronics, automotive, and medical devices. Key players such as Foxconn, Flex Ltd., and Jabil Inc. are leveraging strategic initiatives and technological prowess to meet market demands. Despite challenges like supply chain disruptions, the industry sees opportunities in Industry 4.0 technologies and emerging markets, ensuring continued growth and evolution.

title: Broadcom and SEW-EURODRIVE to Power the Future of Software-Defined Manufacturing
summary: Broadcom and SEW-EURODRIVE are partnering to advance software-defined manufacturing through SEW-EURODRIVE's DriveOperations concept, leveraging VMware Edge Compute Stack (ECS). This collaboration aims to address industrial challenges like scalability and proprietary hardware dependence by enabling rapid and flexible deployment of software applications at the edge. The initiative is expected to enhance operational capabilities, reduce costs, and transform manufacturing productivity.

title: No deal: BHP Group walks away from De Beers acquisition
summary: BHP Group has ended negotiations with Anglo American regarding the acquisition of De Beers Group after Anglo American rejected three previous proposals and the final deadline passed without agreement. BHP cited a lack of access to "key information" during negotiations, while Anglo American expressed concerns about regulatory hurdles in South Africa and the complexity of BHP's offer. Anglo American now plans to divest De Beers and other segments to focus on core areas like copper and iron ore, implying De Beers is still likely to be sold to another suitor.

title: Coherent Corp. names Lattice Semiconductor executive as CEO; shares rise in early trading
summary: Coherent Corp. has appointed Jim Anderson, former CEO of Lattice Semiconductor, as its new chief executive, succeeding Vincent "Chuck" Mattera Jr. Following the announcement, Coherent shares experienced a 16% increase in early trading. Anderson brings extensive semiconductor industry experience from companies like Intel and Advanced Micro Devices, and his compensation package includes $48 million in equity awards.

title: Broadcom, Marvell to 'dominate' custom AI chip market, J.P. Morgan says (NASDAQ:AVGO)
summary: J.P. Morgan predicts that Broadcom and Marvell Technology are poised to dominate the high-end custom AI chip market. The investment firm projects that the application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC) market will grow significantly, reaching between $20 billion and $30 billion. This indicates a strong outlook for both companies in the expanding AI chip sector.

title: 5 things you need to know before nvidia's stock split.
summary: This article details five crucial points regarding NVIDIA's upcoming 1:10 stock split, outlining what a stock split is, its impact on investors and options, historical stock performance post-split, and potential broader market effects. It explains how the split lowers the per-share price to attract more investors and could lead to increased liquidity, while also discussing the proportional adjustment of option contracts. The article also touches upon the "Nvidia Effect" on related stocks and the potential for NVIDIA's split to trigger a wave of similar actions in the US stock market among other high-priced S&P 500 constituents.

title: Study finds fears of post-Broadcom VMware customer flight may be overblown
summary: A new study by CloudBolt Software Inc. and AWS suggests that while many VMware customers are concerned by Broadcom's acquisition, mass defections may be overstated. The survey of 300 IT executives found 76% were concerned, but most are evaluating options rather than immediately migrating. Key concerns include potential price increases and changes to licensing models.

title: Arista releases Etherlink platforms for AI networking focused on large clusters
summary: Arista Networks Inc. announced the launch of its Arista Etherlink AI platforms, designed to improve network performance for AI workloads by supporting large AI clusters with efficient topologies and advanced monitoring. The new platforms, including the 7060X6 AI Leaf, 7800R4 AI Spine, and 7700R4 AI Distributed Etherlink Switch, offer non-blocking throughput and scalability. These developments coincide with Arista's 10th anniversary as a publicly traded company and address the increasing demands of AI networking.

title: Coherent Corp. Gets a New CEO
summary: Coherent Corp. announced James R. Anderson as its new CEO, replacing Vincent D. Mattera, Jr. Anderson's appointment, following his successful tenure at Lattice Semiconductor, caused Coherent's stock to jump 23% while Lattice's fell 15.5%. The move signals Coherent's focus on AI, datacom, and industrial markets, building on Mattera's legacy of significant growth and acquisitions.

title: TSMC-backed VIS joint venture confirms construction of S$10.5 billion chip plant in S'pore
summary: Vanguard International Semiconductor (VIS) and NXP Semiconductors NV are establishing a S$10.5 billion chip plant in Singapore, a joint venture that will begin construction in late 2024 and production in 2027. While not producing cutting-edge chips, the facility will address high demand for automotive and industrial chips, contributing to supply chain diversification and creating 1,500 jobs. Singapore was chosen due to its skilled workforce and previous successful collaborations between the companies.

title: Nand Flash Market size is set to grow by USD 18.8 billion from 2024-2028, Rising demand for nand flash in automotive industry boost the market, Technavio
summary: The global NAND flash market is projected to grow by USD 18.8 billion from 2024-2028, driven by rising demand in the automotive industry and the proliferation of cloud computing services. Technavio reports a CAGR of 5.02% during this period, despite challenges like demand-supply fluctuations. Key players include Samsung, Micron, and Kioxia, with APAC expected to contribute 60% of the market growth.

title: Who are the highest-paid S&P 500 CEOs of 2023?
summary: The median total compensation for S&P 500 CEOs increased by 12.6% in 2023, reaching $16.3 million, largely due to stock awards. Broadcom Inc CEO Hock Tan was the highest-paid chief executive with over $161 million, significantly more than other top earners like Fair Isaac Corporation CEO William Lansing and Apple CEO Tim Cook. This surge in CEO pay outpaced the 5.2% increase for the median S&P 500 employee, further widening the CEO-to-employee pay ratio.

title: Marvell Technology Data Center Revenues Surge, But the Rest Fall
summary: Marvell Technology saw an 87% year-over-year jump in data center revenues, which now account for 70% of its total revenue, driven by the AI infrastructure boom. However, its other segments, including Carrier, Enterprise, Consumer, and Automotive/Industrial, experienced significant declines ranging from 13% to 75%. Despite beating revenue estimates, Marvell's stock dropped 10% due to these segment weaknesses and a conservative outlook, highlighting investor concern over its dependence on data center growth.

title: SOXX: See 'Red' On Semiconductor Stocks, High Risk/High Reward
summary: The semiconductor industry, particularly stocks like Nvidia and Broadcom, has experienced significant price appreciation due to multiple expansions and investor speculation. The SOXX ETF is currently rated "red" for its risk/reward profile, indicating high potential for both upside and major losses. Macroeconomic factors like the US Presidential election and Federal Reserve rate policy could trigger a market-wide selling wave, impacting the semiconductor sector's future performance.

title: Semiconductors Hit Record Highs As Industry Reaches $6.3 Trillion: 5 Chip Stocks That Outpaced Nvidia This Week
summary: The semiconductor industry has reached a record $6.3 trillion valuation, driven by AI advancements, with the PHLX Semiconductor Sector Index showing significant gains. While Nvidia continues to dominate in market capitalization, several other chip stocks, including Broadcom and Arm Holdings, significantly outperformed Nvidia's weekly gains. This indicates a broader bullish trend in the semiconductor sector beyond just the largest players.

title: Broadcom just keeps getting bigger as it’s deemed best AI stock besides Nvidia
summary: Broadcom Inc. passed Eli Lilly & Co. to become the eighth-largest U.S. company by market capitalization after its stock gained 5.4% in Monday's session. This marks a significant ascent for the chip maker, which is increasingly being recognized as a top AI stock alongside Nvidia. The gain also extended Broadcom's winning streak to seven consecutive sessions.

title: Advanced Authentication Market size is set to grow by USD 55.87 billion from 2024-2028, increasing volume of online transactions boost the market, Technavio
summary: The global advanced authentication market is projected to grow by USD 55.87 billion from 2024-2028, driven by the increasing volume of online transactions and the adoption of out-of-band (OOB) authentication, according to Technavio. Despite the challenge of rising OTP token costs, the market is set to accelerate at a CAGR of 19.31%, with North America contributing significantly. The growth is fueled by the need for robust security amidst rising cyber threats and the transition from traditional passwords to multi-factor and biometric authentication solutions.

title: Nvidia set to overtake Apple in $72 billion technology ETF shakeup after its monster 164% rally this year
summary: Nvidia is projected to surpass Apple in the Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) later this month, triggering a significant $23 billion rebalance. This comes after Nvidia's stock surged 164% this year, briefly pushing its valuation past Apple's. The rebalancing is driven by a diversification rule within the ETF that limits the concentration of its top holdings, leading to an estimated $11 billion in Nvidia stock purchases and about $12 billion in Apple stock sales.

title: Here’s why AMD’s stock was just dubbed a top pick

title: Going Under the Hood of Buffer ETFs
