title: Two Indian-origin leaders among top 10 highest-paid S&P 500 CEOs
summary: Two Indian-origin CEOs, Nikesh Arora of Palo Alto Networks Inc and Satya Nadella of Microsoft Corp, are among the top 10 highest-paid S&P 500 company leaders. Arora ranks second with over $151 million, while Nadella is tenth with over $48 million. This highlights a significant disparity between CEO pay, which has surged by over $4 million in a decade, and the average US worker's wage increase of $18,240 during the same period.

title: Broadcom to Host VMware Explore 2024 Barcelona for Customers and Partners
summary: Broadcom Inc. is hosting VMware Explore 2024 Barcelona from November 4-7 at the Fira Gran Via, featuring over 400 sessions, certification training, and an expo. Broadcom President and CEO Hock Tan will deliver a keynote titled “Shaping the Future of Cloud and AI Innovation” on November 5. The event aims to provide attendees with actionable insights into cloud transformation and enterprise workload management.

title: Huawei, Juniper stick out of Gartner’s dwindling SD-WAN competitor pool
summary: Gartner's latest SD-WAN Magic Quadrant report reveals a consolidating market, with a consistent top six leaders and fewer tertiary players. Huawei and Juniper Networks are highlighted for their unique positions outside the leader quadrant; Huawei for its strong sales and market share despite geopolitical limitations, and Juniper for its visionary AI-powered SD-WAN services despite a smaller customer base. The report also notes the increasing importance of SASE strategies and the continued consolidation in the market.

title: Broadcom’s journey to CPO at scale: performance KPIs & fiber reliability
summary: This article details Broadcom's path to CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) at scale. It focuses on the crucial performance KPIs and ensuring fiber reliability within this advanced technology.

title: Defiance Large Cap ex-Mag 7 ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:XMAG)
summary: The Defiance Large Cap ex-Mag 7 ETF (XMAG) tracks the BITA US 500 ex-Magnificent 7 index, offering exposure to large-cap US stocks while excluding the seven largest technology companies to mitigate concentration risk. The ETF, launched on October 21, 2024, has seen its shares increase by 3.5% this year and has surpassed $100 million in assets under management. It maintains a net expense ratio of 0.35% and its top holdings include companies like Broadcom, Eli Lilly, and JPMorgan Chase.

title: Broadcom (AVGO) Gains on Report That It’s Working With OpenAI
summary: Broadcom Inc. shares rose after a Reuters report indicated that OpenAI is collaborating with the company to develop a new artificial intelligence chip. OpenAI is reportedly using Broadcom and TSMC to build an in-house processor to manage its increasing AI computational demands.

title: OpenAI, Broadcom Working to Develop AI Inference Chip
summary: OpenAI is collaborating with Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) to develop a new artificial intelligence chip specifically designed for running AI models after they have been trained, according to sources familiar with the matter. Both companies are also consulting with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) on this effort. Although the discussions are still in early stages, OpenAI has been planning a custom chip and exploring such technology uses for approximately a year.

title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. Just Beat Analyst Forecasts, And Analysts Have Been Updating Their Predictions
summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) recently exceeded analyst forecasts with better-than-expected EPS, despite revenues largely meeting expectations. Following these results, analysts slightly lowered their earnings per share estimates for 2025 but maintained their consensus price target at US$187. AMD is still projected to grow significantly faster than the broader semiconductor industry.

title: Broadcom bolsters VMware Cloud Foundation with storage, compute options
summary: Broadcom has announced significant upgrades to its VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) portfolio, including a 2.5x increase in vSAN capacity to 250 GiB per core and additional compute virtualization options. These enhancements aim to provide customers with a more powerful hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) solution and greater scalability, supporting a growing trend of repatriating workloads back to on-premises and private cloud environments. The updates reflect Broadcom's strategy to innovate VCF for customers embracing the benefits of public cloud within private cloud infrastructure.

title: Broadcom Introduces New Innovations to Accelerate Enterprise IT Modernization
summary: Broadcom announced new innovations, services, and partnerships at VMware Explore 2024 Barcelona to help customers with cloud transformations and AI adoption. Key advancements include expanding VMware Cloud Foundation capabilities for data services and cyber resilience, enhancing VeloCloud for AI networking, and introducing new security solutions like vDefend Intelligent Assist. These initiatives aim to balance the complexities of IT modernization with the imperative to adopt generative AI while managing operational costs and security risks.

title: Did This AI Chip Giant Just Score a Major Victory Over Nvidia?
summary: Broadcom Inc. is emerging as a significant challenger to Nvidia's dominance in the AI chip market, driven by its advancements in AI-driven chips and strategic partnerships. A recent partnership with OpenAI to develop custom in-house chips specifically for AI inference needs further solidifies Broadcom's position. Despite a recent dip in stock value due to conservative Q4 guidance, analysts remain highly bullish on AVGO stock, anticipating significant upside potential.

title: Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) Services Market to Reach USD 77.90 Billion by 2032 - Report by S&S Insider
summary: The Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) Services Market was valued at USD 40.10 Billion in 2023 and is projected to reach USD 77.90 Billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 7.67%. The growth is driven by increasing complexity of semiconductor devices and demand for cost-effective manufacturing, with Taiwan, China, and the U.S. dominating market share. Despite a decline in 2023 due to reduced consumer electronics demand, advancements in packaging technologies and recovery in semiconductor demand are expected to fuel growth from 2024 onwards.

title: Is First Trust NASDAQ Semiconductor ETF (FTXL) a Strong ETF Right Now?
summary: The article analyzes the First Trust NASDAQ Semiconductor ETF (FTXL), highlighting its smart beta strategy and performance. It notes FTXL's significant asset size ($1.45 billion), expense ratio of 0.60%, and strong year-to-date and 12-month returns of 16.05% and 40.77% respectively. The fund's primary sector exposure is Information Technology, with Intel, Broadcom, and Applied Materials as top holdings.

title: Broadcom (AVGO) Q4 & FY 2024 Results Schedule: GuruFocus Value and Potential Price Movement
summary: Broadcom (AVGO) has scheduled its Q4 and fiscal year 2024 financial results release for December 12, 2024, after market close, with a subsequent conference call. The article highlights past EPS misses and varying price movements, alongside analyst and GuruFocus price targets. Analysts project an average upside of +12.06%, while GuruFocus estimates a downside of -30.91%.

title: EchoStar removes going concern disclosure, but DirecTV deal in jeopardy
summary: EchoStar has removed its "going concern" disclosure after securing critical financing, alleviating bankruptcy concerns. However, its proposed deal to sell its video business to DirecTV for $1 and the assumption of $9.75 billion in debt is in jeopardy after Dish bondholders rejected a debt exchange offer. Despite this setback, EchoStar plans to continue with its open RAN 5G network buildout and other business segments.

title: SK Telecom to acquire additional 24.76% stake in SK Broadband
summary: SK Telecom plans to acquire an additional 24.76 percent stake in its subsidiary SK Broadband from Taekwang Group and Mirae Asset Financial Group for 1.15 trillion won by May 2025. This acquisition will increase SKT's stake in SK Broadband to 99.1 percent, aiming to strengthen its presence in data centers and submarine cable investments to advance its AI capabilities. The move is part of SKT's broader strategy to invest heavily in the AI sector and enhance operational efficiency across all business sectors.

title: Broadcom’s VMware strategy is winning despite market friction
summary: Broadcom's acquisition of VMware is succeeding despite media and competitor narratives focusing on increased license fees. The strategy involves narrowing VMware's focus, increasing contract values through the VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) bundle, and integrating VMware into Broadcom’s business model. While some customers are migrating low-value workloads, most mission-critical work remains, leading to significant revenue increases and a long-term win for customers, competitors, and Broadcom.

title: Lightmatter and Amkor Technology Partner to Build World’s Largest 3D Photonics Package
summary: Lightmatter and Amkor Technology have announced a strategic partnership to develop the largest-ever 3D-packaged chip complex using Lightmatter's Passage™ platform. This collaboration aims to overcome the interconnect scaling and power demands of AI workloads by integrating Lightmatter's 3D-stacked photonic engine with Amkor’s advanced multi-die packaging expertise. The combined solution promises higher connection density, bandwidth, and energy efficiency, enabling significant advancements in AI and high-performance computing.

title: Should You Investigate Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMD) At US$139?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) has seen significant price movements, with its stock currently trading at US$139 after reaching US$173 and dropping to US$129. Simply Wall St's valuation suggests an intrinsic value of $213.12, indicating the stock is undervalued, despite its volatility. With expected profit growth doubling over the next few years, the future outlook for AMD appears bright, presenting a potential buying opportunity for investors.

title: Platinum Investment Management Reduces Stake in Broadcom Inc.
summary: Platinum Investment Management, managed by Marcia Venegas, recently reduced its stake in Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) by 70,072 shares. TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, rates AVGO as "Outperform" due to strong financial performance, despite concerns about potential overbought conditions and a high P/E ratio.

title: Applied Materials Announces New Collaboration Model for Advanced Packaging at Summit on Energy-Efficient Computing
summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has announced an expansion of its global EPIC innovation platform to accelerate the commercialization of advanced chip packaging technologies, specifically targeting energy-efficient computing for AI. The company convened over two dozen semiconductor R&D leaders in Singapore to foster collaboration across the industry. This initiative, EPIC Advanced Packaging, aims to address challenges in developing next-generation packaging technologies by facilitating co-innovation and providing early access to new equipment and expertise.

title: AI factories are requiring better cooling, more efficient systems
summary: The article discusses the collaboration between Denvr Dataworks Inc., Broadcom Inc., and Dell Technologies Inc. to address the challenges of building efficient AI factories that require massive compute power. They are focusing on better cooling systems, such as liquid immersion and liquid-to-chip cooling, and open networking solutions like Ethernet to handle the power-hungry AI workloads. The goal is to create modular, interoperable, and scalable AI infrastructure with efficient power usage and a compact footprint, supported by a full software AI stack.

title: AMOLED Displays: Worldwide Market Trends, Business Opportunities and Competitive Intelligence (2025-2030) Featuring Samsung Electronics, LG, BOE Technology Group & More
summary: The AMOLED Display Market is projected to grow from USD 14.99 billion in 2024 to USD 28.71 billion by 2030, at a CAGR of 11.34%. This growth is driven by advancements in display technology, improved production capabilities, and increased adoption of IoT devices. The report highlights key market trends, growth opportunities, challenges, and competitive landscapes, featuring major players like Samsung Electronics, LG, and BOE Technology Group.

title: Grid Dynamics Introduces Composable Commerce Starter Kit on Microsoft Azure, Enabling Rapid Replacement of Legacy Systems with Modern Digital Commerce Capabilities
summary: Grid Dynamics has launched its Composable Commerce Starter Kit on Microsoft Azure, designed to help businesses rapidly replace legacy systems with modern digital commerce capabilities. This kit allows companies to deploy a minimum viable product (MVP) in months and leverage best-of-breed commerce components to build flexible, custom digital platforms. The solution, built on MACH architecture principles, integrates with commercetools and offers pre-built connectors for third-party ISVs, enabling businesses to enhance customer experiences and drive operational efficiency, according to Grid Dynamics and its partners.

title: AI networking solutions drive scalable, efficient systems
summary: Broadcom is leading the charge in AI networking solutions, focusing on energy-efficient chips and scalable networks to meet the escalating demands of AI and high-performance computing. At SC24, Broadcom leaders highlighted innovations like the Tomahawk 5 chip and Thor 2 NIC, which significantly reduce power consumption while maintaining performance. The company emphasizes collaboration with partners like Dell Technologies Inc. and Denver Dataworks Corp. to create open, scalable systems with Ethernet as the foundational "glue" for managing complex AI clusters.

title: HPE announces VM Essentials – the VMWare competitor that isn’t
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) unveiled Virtual Machine Essentials (VME) at HPE Discover Barcelona 2024, presenting it as an alternative for customers wary of VMware's recent pricing changes. While HPE executives avoided directly positioning VME as a competitor, they highlighted its ability to lower virtualization costs and offer flexibility, especially for those who don't need all the features of VMware. VME is built on Morpheus technology and can manage existing VMware workloads alongside its own hypervisor, aiming to provide a simplified VM management experience.

title: Monolithic Power Stock: Buy, Sell, or Hold?
summary: Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) stock has significantly underperformed the S&P 500 in 2024, experiencing a sharp sell-off after Q3 earnings and concerns about reduced orders from a key customer, Nvidia. Despite these setbacks and mixed analyst sentiment, the stock is considered a "Strong Buy" overall, with a mean price target suggesting substantial upside. Investors are grappling with whether recent drops fully price in performance and order-related risks.

title: Samsung Ends Antitrust Dispute with Broadcom After Five Months
summary: Samsung Electronics has dropped its federal antitrust lawsuit against Broadcom Inc. five months after it was initiated. The lawsuit alleged that Broadcom, a cellphone chip supplier, used unlawful "tying arrangements" to restrict Samsung's ability to source components from other manufacturers. The two companies have resolved all litigation, though details of any settlement remain undisclosed.

title: 10 Best Stocks to Buy For Long Term Growth
summary: This article highlights Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ:AVGO) as a strong contender among the best stocks for long-term growth, citing its focus on AI technologies, strategic partnerships, and projected revenue growth from AI. ClearBridge Investments also expressed confidence in Broadcom's custom silicon business and the acquisition of VMware for durable, subscription-based growth. The article emphasizes Broadcom's economic moat through groundbreaking technologies and its trajectory to becoming a technology powerhouse.

title: How MRAM Is enhancing reliability in medical devices
summary: MRAM is emerging as a critical technology for enhancing reliability in medical devices, offering persistent, high-speed memory that ensures data integrity even during power loss. It leverages magnetic states for data storage, eliminating the need for backup power and providing virtually infinite write endurance, crucial for continuous data logging in life-critical systems like hemodialysis machines. By bridging the gaps of traditional memory solutions, MRAM improves performance, reliability, and safety in healthcare technology, leading to long-term cost savings for manufacturers.

title: 25 Best Stocks to Own in December, Including Broadcom
summary: Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ: AVGO) is highlighted as one of the 25 best stocks to own in December, based on its historical performance of finishing the month higher eight out of the past ten years with an average gain of 7.3%. Despite a 4.5% loss in November, the stock is showing signs of rebound, is in "oversold" territory according to its 14-day RSI, and maintains long-term support. The equity is up 50.1% year-to-date.

title: Broadcom Earnings: Will AI Revenue Spark Gains or Byte-Sized Losses?
summary: This article examines Broadcom Inc.'s upcoming Q4 earnings report, focusing on the potential impact of its AI revenue, the integration of its VMware acquisition, and future guidance. Analysts anticipate increased net income driven by demand for custom AI chips and Ethernet networking, with AI-related sales for fiscal 2024 projected at $12 billion. The report discusses both bullish and bearish scenarios depending on the company's performance in these key areas and suggests leveraged ETFs for traders looking to capitalize on potential stock movements.

title: Broadcom Inc. Launches 3.5D eXtreme Dimension System in Package (XDSiP) Platform for Custom AI Accelerators
summary: Broadcom Inc. has launched its 3.5D eXtreme Dimension System in Package (XDSiP) technology, a new platform for creating custom AI accelerators (XPUs). This platform integrates over 6000 mm² of silicon and up to 12 high-bandwidth memory stacks, significantly enhancing performance, power efficiency, and integration capabilities for AI applications, including generative AI models. Broadcom, in collaboration with TSMC and Fujitsu, anticipates production shipments to begin in February 2026, marking a crucial advancement in high-performance computing.

title: Apple plans three-year modem rollout to compete with Qualcomm, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Apple is preparing to launch its own series of cellular modem chips starting next year, aiming to replace components from Qualcomm by 2027. This move intensifies competition with Qualcomm, which currently supplies modems to Apple, and could impact other Apple suppliers like Skyworks Solutions and Qorvo. Apple has been developing its own modem technology since acquiring Intel's modem unit in 2019 and recently signed a deal with Broadcom for 5G radio frequency components.

title: AI's growth is just getting started, BlackRock's thematic ETF head says
summary: BlackRock's U.S. head of thematic and active ETFs, Jay Jacobs, believes the AI boom is still in its early stages, highlighting infrastructure and cybersecurity as key areas for growth in 2025. He emphasizes that the theme extends beyond megacap tech to include various semiconductor, data center, and software companies. Jacobs also points out the need for physical infrastructure, including energy, materials, and real estate, to support AI's expansion.

title: Why Marvell Technology is the next AI winner
summary: Marvell Technology has seen its market value nearly double in the past year, largely due to its significant role in the booming AI infrastructure market. The company partners with all hyperscalers, particularly AWS, providing custom AI chips and infrastructure solutions. While not consistently profitable, its strategic positioning in AI has driven its stock market performance, with expectations to convert this into future profits.

title: Broadcom Announces New Enterprise-Grade Bitnami Offering
summary: Broadcom announced the introduction of Bitnami Premium, a commercial enterprise version of Bitnami by VMware Tanzu, addressing the need for enhanced security and support for open source software. The company also named Arrow Electronics as a global distributor for Bitnami Premium, a move aimed at expanding its reach and streamlining the purchasing and support experience for users. Bitnami Premium offers enterprise support, up-to-date LTS branches, unlimited container pulls, and secure software supply chain metadata.

title: STMicro launches 'edge' AI microcontroller
summary: STMicroelectronics, a major European chip manufacturer, has released its new STM32N6 series of microcontrollers, designed for edge AI and machine learning applications. These chips will enable consumer and industrial devices to perform image and audio processing locally, reducing the need for large data centers. This approach to AI saves time and electricity by processing data at the source, rather than sending it to remote servers.

title: Vultr Collaborates with AMD, Broadcom and Juniper Networks to Pioneer New GPU Data Center Architecture
summary: Vultr has announced a strategic four-way collaboration with AMD, Broadcom, and Juniper Networks to create a new GPU data center architecture. This initiative includes expanding Vultr's Chicago data center with an AMD GPU supercompute cluster powered by ROCm™ open software. The collaboration aims to provide enterprises and AI innovators with high-performance, scalable, and secure cloud GPU infrastructure for global AI training and inference workloads

title: Report: Apple working with Broadcom to develop custom chipset for AI
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly collaborating with Broadcom Inc. to develop a custom server processor, codenamed “Baltra,” for AI features in its iOS operating system, with a targeted launch in 2026. This move aligns with Apple's strategy of using proprietary chips for on-device AI and Broadcom's expertise in semiconductor interconnect designs. The project likely leverages Broadcom's advanced packaging technologies for faster chip-to-chip communications.

title: RF Front-End Chip Market Skyrockets to $47.10 Billion by 2030 | CAGR of 12.4%, Dominated by Tech Giants - Broadcom Corporation, Infineon Technologies AG and Microchip Technology Inc. | The Insight Partners
summary: The global RF front-end chip market is projected to reach $47.10 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 12.4%, driven by the increasing adoption of advanced wireless infrastructure and consumer electronics. Key drivers include the miniaturization of RF front-end modules and the emergence of 5G technology, with major contributions from companies like Broadcom Corporation, Infineon Technologies AG, and Microchip Technology Inc. Asia Pacific is expected to dominate this market.

title: Broadcom Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2024 Financial Results and Quarterly Dividend
summary: Broadcom Inc. reported strong financial results for its fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024, with revenue reaching $14,054 million in Q4 and $51.6 billion for the full fiscal year. The company's semiconductor and infrastructure software segments both contributed significantly to growth, with AI-driven revenue being a major factor. Broadcom also announced an 11% increase in its quarterly common stock dividend to $0.59 per share for fiscal year 2025.

title: Broadcom ETFs Surge on Strong AI Demand
summary: Broadcom's shares surged by 20% following strong earnings and optimistic projections for its AI business, pushing its market cap past $1 trillion. This surge significantly boosted ETFs with large Broadcom holdings, such as the Defiance Daily Target 2X Long AVGO ETF (AVGX) and the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX). The demand for Broadcom's custom AI chips, favored by major tech companies like Alphabet, Meta, and Apple, is seen as a key driver for its continued growth.

title: Broadcom's AI Chip Leadership Puts Pressure on Nvidia and Marvell, Analysts Say
summary: Broadcom's stock surged after reporting strong Q4 adjusted EPS results and raising its dividend, driven by its leadership in AI custom Application-Specific Integrated Circuits (ASICs). Analysts upgraded Broadcom's price targets, highlighting its expanding AI opportunities and advanced 3nm silicon technology, which puts it ahead of competitors like Nvidia and Marvell. The company anticipates significant growth in its AI revenues, expecting to reach $30 billion by calendar 2027 based on strong demand from hyperscalers.

title: Broadcom's long and winding path to the trillion-dollar club, and how Trump played a role
summary: Broadcom recently joined the trillion-dollar market cap club, driven by a 24% stock surge after strong earnings and optimistic guidance. The company’s journey included a thwarted 2018 acquisition of Qualcomm, blocked by the Trump administration over security concerns, which ironically led Broadcom to pivot towards software acquisitions like CA Technologies, Symantec, and VMware. This strategic shift, particularly into infrastructure software and custom AI chips for hyperscale customers, has fueled its significant growth despite not keeping pace with Nvidia in the AI boom.

title: Nvidia's Blackwell chip could push the company into a new stratosphere as the AI revolution continues
summary: Nvidia's Blackwell chip is poised to further propel the company's growth in the AI sector, with analysts calling it a "beast" and an "exponential leap forward." Despite potential future competition in inference workloads, Blackwell is expected to dominate the AI training market and contribute significantly to Nvidia's record sales, even as demand continues to outstrip supply for several quarters.

title: After Earnings, Is Broadcom Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Broadcom's third-quarter earnings report, released on December 12, 2024, has led Morningstar to raise its fair value estimate for the company's stock to $190 per share, up from $155, driven by an increased AI revenue growth forecast. Despite the upward revision, Morningstar now considers Broadcom's shares to be roughly fairly valued after a significant post-earnings jump, giving it a 2-star rating. The article highlights Broadcom's strong potential in AI chips, its wide economic moat from intangible assets and switching costs, and its financial strength, while also noting risks related to market cycles and supply chain reliance on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing.

title: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) reports earnings
summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) has filed its annual 10-K report for the fiscal year ended November 3, 2024, detailing its $30.8 billion acquisition of VMware, Inc. The company reported a significant increase in total net revenue to $51.6 billion, up 44% from the previous year, with contributions from both semiconductor solutions and infrastructure software segments. Despite revenue growth, net income decreased to $5.9 billion due to higher amortization and restructuring charges related to the acquisition.

title: Is Microchip Technology (NASDAQ:MCHP) Using Too Much Debt?
summary: This article analyzes Microchip Technology's debt situation, noting its net debt of US$6.14b as of September 2024. While the company has substantial liabilities, its market capitalization of US$30.1b suggests it could manage potential issues. The analysis concludes that while debt makes MCHP somewhat risky, its ability to convert EBIT to free cash flow is a positive factor.

title: Broadcom CEO Hock Tan claims he has no interest in buying Intel
summary: Broadcom CEO Hock Tan has stated he is not interested in acquiring Intel Corp., focusing instead on making Broadcom an AI powerhouse. Tan cited his company's efforts to increase market share in the AI chip sector and his reluctance to pursue hostile takeovers after a past failed attempt with Qualcomm. This news comes as Broadcom's market capitalization has soared due to AI enthusiasm, while Intel's value has significantly declined.

title: BAOS Stock Soars to 52-Week High, Hits $7.33 Amid Bullish Run
summary: Baosheng Media Group Holdings Limited (BAOS) stock has reached a 52-week high of $7.33. The micro-cap company, with a valuation of $10 million and strong liquidity, has seen a 10.19% gain in the past week, though InvestingPro analysis suggests it is significantly overvalued.

title: Netflix files suit against Broadcom over VMware virtual machine patents
summary: Netflix Inc. is suing Broadcom Inc. over five patents related to virtual machines, which Netflix claims are being infringed upon by Broadcom's subsidiary, VMware Inc. The lawsuit, filed in a California federal court, alleges willful infringement by VMware since 2012 and seeks unspecified damages. This legal action adds to an existing patent dispute between Netflix and Broadcom concerning video streaming technology.

title: 3 Stocks With Rising Prices That Are Likely to Split in 2025
summary: The article identifies Broadcom (AVGO), Casey's General Stores (CASY), and Costco (COST) as companies whose stock prices are high enough to warrant a stock split in 2025. Stock splits make shares more affordable, increase market liquidity, and signal company confidence, often leading to outperformance. The author details the financial health and growth drivers for each company, reinforcing the likelihood of future splits.

title: Nvidia Supplier Ibiden Weighs Faster Expansion for AI Demand
summary: Ibiden Co., a key supplier of chip package substrates for Nvidia, is considering accelerating its production capacity expansion due to robust demand from the AI sector. CEO Koji Kawashima indicated that current expansion plans might not be sufficient to meet customer needs, prompting discussions for further investment. This expansion comes as Ibiden's shares rose and despite past reliance on Intel, the company is seeing increased demand driven by AI chips used by Nvidia and potentially other chipmakers.

title: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) Poised for Strong 2025 Growth with Custom AI Chips and Strategic Partnerships, Says William Blair
summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) is anticipated to experience strong growth in 2025, driven by its custom AI chip development and strategic partnerships with major cloud customers, according to William Blair. William Blair maintains an Outperform rating, expecting steady growth from existing partnerships and potential new clients like ByteDance, OpenAI, and Apple.

title: Experts Think a Broadcom Stock Split Is Coming in 2025. Should You Buy AVGO Now?
summary: Experts anticipate another Broadcom (AVGO) stock split in 2025 following a significant rally in 2024 driven by its AI and semiconductor performance. Broadcom's strong fundamentals, including impressive revenue and earnings growth, high free cash flow, and strategic advancements in AI and edge computing, reinforce its appeal. Analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating for AVGO stock, signaling confidence in its continued growth trajectory.

title: Samsung, Texas Instruments, and Amkor finalize CHIPS Act funding agreements
summary: Samsung, Texas Instruments, and Amkor have finalized their CHIPS and Science Act funding agreements with the US Department of Commerce. Samsung will receive $4.745 billion for its $37 billion investment in Texas, Texas Instruments will get $1.61 billion for its $18 billion project across Texas and Utah, and Amkor will receive $407 million for its $2 billion investment in an Arizona OSAT plant. These agreements are part of a broader effort by the US to secure a domestic supply of advanced semiconductors and are finalized before a new presidential term which may see changes to CHIPS Act support.

title: Most of the ‘Sweet 16’ stocks have dominated. They might do it again in 2025.
summary: The article discusses the "Sweet 16" stocks identified by Jefferies in September 2020, highlighting their significant contribution to the US stock market's performance over the past five years. It suggests these stocks, including Nvidia, may continue their dominant trend into 2025 due to expected revenue growth. The piece also mentions other dominant stock groupings like "BATMMAAN" and the "Magnificent Seven."

title: Most of the ‘Sweet 16’ stocks have dominated. They might do it again in 2025.
summary: The article discusses the "Sweet 16" list of stocks, initially identified by Jefferies in September 2020, highlighting their significant contribution to the U.S. stock market's performance. title: Industry Leaders Applaud New National Semiconductor Prototyping And R&D Facility In Arizona
summary: The U.S. Commerce Department has selected Arizona and ASU as the site for the third CHIPS for America research and development (R&D) flagship facility, bolstering Arizona's semiconductor ecosystem. Industry leaders from various companies and academic institutions are praising this decision, highlighting its importance for strengthening the domestic supply chain, driving advanced manufacturing, and securing America's leadership in the semiconductor industry. The facility is expected to be operational by 2028 and will serve as a hub for exploring and collaborating on next-generation semiconductor technologies.

summary: STMicroelectronics (STM) stock has seen a nearly 50% drop from its 2024 high due to a slowdown in industrial chip demand, yet it remains highly profitable with a strong balance sheet. Market observers suggest last year's AI leaders, which are heavily represented in QBIG, could extend their dominance this year.

title: Broadcom Inc: AI-Powered Stock Analysis & Analyst Coverage Update

Broadcom has begun notifying affected current and former staff, advising them to monitor financial records and enable multi-factor authentication.

It details design strategies, key technologies like MMICs and MCMs, and crucial components such as tuners and filters, concluding with future trends like silicon photonics and quantum-assisted signal processing. Darren Hintze of Mercury Systems emphasizes the need for adaptable, efficient, and cost-effective RF front-end solutions.

title: DAX: Europas strahlender Stern – doch wie lange noch?
summary: The DAX has significantly outperformed global indices like the S&P 500 and MSCI World, driven by fiscal impulses, anticipated ECB rate cuts, and capital reallocation. The iShares Core DAX UCITS ETF is highlighted as an efficient way for investors to participate in this trend.

Despite this, the company's shares fell approximately 7% due to disappointment in its software segment, which underperformed revenue estimates, causing investor frustration. IBM maintained its full-year revenue growth projection of at least 5% and also raised its free cash flow outlook for 2025.

title: STMicroelectronics’ $950M MEMS Deal: A Strategic Reset in Turbulent Times

The deal is expected to be accretive for ST earnings per share from completion, projected in H1 2026, positioning the company for significant growth in key segments like intelligent sensors and next-gen automotive systems.

Bitdeer is also advancing its HPC/AI initiatives at its Clarington site and developing advanced A3 and CLO4 chips for improved efficiency.

Additionally, they are innovating in Gallium Nitride (GaN) technology at GF's Vermont facility, aiming to enhance Cirrus Logic's mixed-signal and power technology portfolio and strengthen the U.S. semiconductor supply chain.

Key benefits include enterprise-grade support for Ubuntu OS, enhanced security and efficiency with chiseled containers, and faster AI deployments through precompiled virtualized GPU drivers.

Broadcom is reinvesting nearly 20% of VMware's revenue into R&D, streamlining market routes, and requiring partners to offer full-stack VCF as a service, moving beyond mere licensing to integrated solutions.
